source.messages:
Ava Morales: heads-up — webinar signups are getting delayed by enrichment; we keep missing the 1st-touch window.
Ethan Park: ugh. can we do immediate routing + enrichment-as-patch? propose 5m acceptance SLA for the event queue.
Ava Morales: yes. plan: add webhook flag quick_route=true and owner_policy=preserve. HubSpot workflow: if quick_route==true -> round-robin to 'web-event-sdr' and set owner_preserve metadata. route immediately, then apply enrichment patch when ready.
Priya Shah: safe on our side. enrichment job will PATCH only profile fields if owner != null. sample patch payload (escaping creds): {"email":"test@acme.com","profile_complete":"false","lead_score":"42"}. we will NOT touch owner id unless owner==null.
Noah Rivera: fallback: if no accept within 5m -> escalate to 'sdr-overflow' and ping #marketing-sdr. also add slack note: "auto-escalated after 5m no-accept".
lead-bot: webhook smoke ok (200). sample incoming: {"email":"attendee@org.com","quick_route":"true","campaign":"spring-demo-q2"}.
Ethan Park: canary plan: start 3% of registrations with x-rollout=canary header. Monitor: acceptance_rate, median accept_time_ms, owner_change_count.
Priya Shah: also touching SF connector: when HubSpot enrichment upserts into Salesforce, pass preserve_owner=true so SF workflow doesn't reassign. will add mapping rule and a unit test.
Ava Morales: SDR playbook notes: when profile shows profile_complete=false -> 1) verify email/role, 2) call with intro script A, 3) update note "partial profile: follow up". I'll post the short script in #marketing.
Noah Rivera: alert thresholds: median accept_time_ms > 300000 (5m) OR owner_change_rate > 0.05 (5%) in 30m window -> rollback canary and page oncall.
Ethan Park: timeline: implement routing flag & hubspot workflow today, canary tomorrow 09:00, ramp to 25% after 2h if all green.
Ava Morales: great. Priya -- can you confirm SF mapping once done? tagging you. :thumbsup:

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Heads up — planning to ship the onboarding welcome drip (3 emails) next Tues. Goal: convert free tier -> active usage. Draft campaign in Figma: https://figma.com/file/xyz. Need final subject lines and APAC send windows.
Diego Morales: Nice. What's the send strategy for APAC? one global send at 09:00 PT or staggered by local time?
Ava Chen: Stagger — want local 9–11am where possible. Ops asked for spike throttling. Proposal: three regional windows (APAC, EMEA, Americas) with 4h padding for overlap.
Priya Singh: For deliverability, prefer smaller batches for APAC ISPs. Can we split APAC into ANZ + SEA + EAS? rough buckets 50k / 75k / 40k.
Samir Patel: Agree. Also add seed list + Gmail inboxing check and a 10k holdback for warmup. I'll add these to the campaign config.
Maya Liu: Subject lines A/B — (A) "Welcome to Redwood — quick setup in 3 steps" (B) "Start generating: your Redwood quickstart" — I lean B, more action-oriented.
Diego Morales: B feels better for activation. Save A for re-engagement sends later.
Priya Singh: Also add a plain-text fallback for heavy-mobile recipients and reduce image payload. Mobile rendering has been a weak signal in previous sends.
Ava Chen: Great. @Samir can you add a send throttle policy: ramp 0->25% in first 30m, 50% in 2h, full after 6h. And set retry on 429 with exponential backoff. Example config snippet: ```send_policy: ramp: [0.25@30m, 0.5@2h, 1@6h] retry: {on: [429], strategy: exponential}```
Samir Patel: On it. Adding a 5% canary across all regions first, then progressive rollout. Will post campaign IDs when ready.
deliverability-bot: deploy scheduled: campaign "onboard-welcome-2026-03" planned for 2026-04-06T14:00:00Z. Seeds passing: gmail, outlook. Spam-trap check: clear. Note: template v1 flagged for high image ratio.
Maya Liu: Can we soften the header copy — currently reads too "enterprise" for our target dev audience. Suggest swap "enterprise-grade" -> "developer-first".
Ava Chen: Ack. I’ll push that copy edit into the doc now. Final ask: QA checklist before launch — https://docs.internal/checklists/onboard-email. Need approvals from @Priya and @Samir by EOD Fri.
Priya Singh: I’ll review tonight and flag any deliverability concerns.
Diego Morales: UTM structure confirmed: source=newsletter, medium=email, campaign=onboard_q2. Redirects via tracker; will export final CSV of recipient buckets.
Ava Chen: Perfect. Locking this thread as the single source of truth for this campaign. :rocket:

source.thread:
Maya Chen: Draft for the Optimize launch post is up. Need: quick tone pass, legal on benchmarks, and final HubSpot CTA + lead routing. Doc: https://docs.google.com/d/optimize-launch-draft

Alex Rivera: on it. I'll drop 3 hero options and short social lines. Keep the hero benefit-first please.

Priya Das: i'll do the body tone sweep. also suggest a 1-line customer quote placeholder above benchmarks.

Maya Chen: added placeholder quote and a short appendix with test harness. Current bench language: "20-40% lower per-token cost with caching + batching".

legal-bot: Automated check: comparative claim flagged. Please rephrase to include test context or say "typical" range + footnote (model/hardware/seq).

Maya Chen: switching to: "Typical reductions of ~20-40% in per-token cost observed in internal tests; results vary by model and prompt length." will add footnote with params.

Sam Torres: Ops asks if we should publish an example throughput table per GPU. I'm ok with an example table marked as "typical".

Jordan Blake: agree. Add caveat: results vary by prompt composition and sequence length.

crm-bot: HubSpot form id: 55521. CTA link should include utm_source=blog, utm_medium=post, utm_campaign=optimize_launch_q2. Suggested URL: https://redwood.com/optimize?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=post&utm_campaign=optimize_launch_q2

Maya Chen: adding UTMs. For lead routing, propose this pseudocode to copy to ops playbook:
```
# HubSpot routing rules
if attended and (watched_pct >= 50 or demo_requested == true):
  assign_to: sales-sdr
  tags: [optimize_launch, webinar_hot]
  priority: high
elif registered:
  nurture_flow: webinar_nurture
  tags: [optimize_launch]
```

Alex Rivera: hero pick: "Cut inference costkeep output quality." (I'll tighten punctuation in doc) :+1:

Priya Das: added short social shareables and a TL;DR for the post. Also removed a sentence that sounded like a guarantee.

legal-bot: Footnote requested: model=llama-3-13b, seq_len_p95=512, batching=dynamic, runs=10, hardware=A100-80GB. Avoid absolute language.

Maya Chen: footnote added, ops table labeled as example, CTAs updated. Plan: request legal quick-pass after this edit; if approved we'll request publish slot for tomorrow 11:00 PT. Any other blockers?

Sam Torres: none here. I'll validate throughput numbers with infra runbook and confirm.

Jordan Blake: i'll ping AE for a real quote to replace placeholder.

Maya Chen: thanks all :rocket:

source.messages:
Maya Patel: Quick check — rehearsal swap proposal. I need to swap demo 2 (interactive routing) from Jasper -> Ava due to a conflict. Can we confirm mic/slide handoff?
Connor Blake: Fine by me, thanks for the heads up. Ava, you good taking the live demo slot at 10:25 PT? :calendar:
Ava Chen: Yep I can take it. Will need the demo tokens and the shortened prompt set by EOD so I can rehearse with the actual model.
Maya Patel: Sending the prompt pack now. Also note: red-team plan — we want a fallback model and an automated failover script in case the hosted endpoint spikes.
Jasper Reed: For fallback use: redwood-dedicated-small (quantized). It's compatible with the demo prompts and faster cold-start. I'll drop the curl and a tiny healthcheck script below.
Jasper Reed: ```bash
# healthcheck (polls live endpoint)
STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.redwood.ai/v1/health)
if [ "$STATUS" -ne 200 ]; then
  # switch to dedicated fallback
  echo "Failing over to redwood-dedicated-small"
  # call routing api to swap
fi
```
Li Wang: OpsBot, please create a quick alert in #temp-alerts when latency > 600ms for the demo route. Tag: @marketing
OpsBot: Alert rule created: channel=#temp-alerts threshold=600ms route=webinar-demo-2026-04-02
Connor Blake: Logistics: Ava will share screen, Jasper will monitor healthchecks and be ready to trigger the failover, Maya will moderate Qs. We should run one full run at 09:30 PT on the day.
Ava Chen: Request: can someone confirm the token quotas for the rehearsal endpoint? I need at least 50k tokens to run multi-pass scenarios.
Maya Patel: Budget approved for rehearsal tokens. Connor I'll send you the billing scope. Also, recording note: please record both system audio and presenter mic separately for edit.
Jasper Reed: I'll be on standby for the 09:30 dry run and the main show. Quick test link for everyone: https://staging.redwood.ai/webinar-demo (use acct: webinar-test)
Connor Blake: Perfect. If no objections I'll lock the run-of-show with the swap and post the final cue sheet by 6pm PT. :thumbsup:
Li Wang: +1
Ava Chen: Thanks — I'll start rehearsing with the prompt pack now.

source.messages:
Riley Park: Idea: 20-min mini-lab + 20-min Q&A. Bundle = 1 short tutorial, a tiny refapp, and a live bench demo. Goal: dev signups + repo stars.
Jordan Blake: 👍 timeline?
Riley Park: Target May 24 webinar. Tutorial publish May 17, repo open May 20, promo starts May 10.
Maya Ortiz: I can write the tutorial (hands-on, <700 words). Will include quick run steps: clone, set API key, run demo.
Chris Wang: I'll seed repo `community-mini-stream` with bench.py. Example to include in tutorial:
```
python bench.py --model community-stream --seq 512 --requests 30 --warmup 5
```
Outputs p50/p95 and tokens/sec.
Jordan Blake: Landing CTA = RSVP (email, role, company_size). Post-event CTA: repo + free hosted trial link. Add hidden `interest` (Hosted/Dedicated/Private).
Ops Bot: webhook test -> /integrations/hubspot/mini-lab -> ok.
Maya Ortiz: Messaging angle: "replayable benchmarks" + "build a streaming chat in 10 lines". I'll draft 3 headlines for A/B.
Riley Park: Backup plan: pre-recorded bench clip and fallback model `community-stream-small`.
Chris Wang: Ops note: reserve a small Dedicated slot for demo window. I'll coordinate.
Jordan Blake: Asset ask: hero image + 7s GIF. Design deliver by May 15.
Maya Ortiz: I'll drop draft May 12 for review. Chris/Jordan can review by May 14.
Riley Park: Perfect. Adding tasks to marketing board under "mini-lab: streaming + bench". Thumbs if good. :thumbsup:
Riley Park: :thumbsup:
Jordan Blake: :thumbsup:
Maya Ortiz: :thumbsup:
Chris Wang: :thumbsup:
Ops Bot: thread archived for tracking.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — the weekly digest shows a weird spike in inbound MQLs from the EU cohort this morning; chart: https://pulseboard.redwood/series/launch-ops?range=24h
Ben Ortiz: saw that. Source filter looks like 'webinar-signup' but CVR is low. Could be duped UTM params or fb redirect issue :thinking_face:
Samir Patel: i'm pulling raw event rows now. quick ask — which dashboard query did you use? we have 3 slightly different cohort windows.
Ava Chen: used 'Launch > Paid + Org-webinar' on the pulseboard. Timestamped at 08:13 UTC. I'll paste the widget config later.
Lina Gomez: tagging in case analytics can confirm: @mkt-analytics any chance a campaign tag change rolled out overnight?

SELECT count(*) FROM events WHERE event='signup' AND created_at >= '2026-03-22' AND (utm_source='unknown-web') AND region='eu';

Ava Chen: ugh — so these are inflating paid-webinar numbers. we need to correct attribution and update the pulseboard widget to exclude unknown-web or map it to organic.

source.messages:
Maya Patel: quick idea — run a narrow PLG microtest pairing a 25m "Starter Sprint" workshop + 6 starter prompts + small credits to nudge first-gen and trial starts.
Jon Rivera: sample & arms?
Maya Patel: ~4.2k recent signups (0–12d). three arms: control, email + 6 credits + replay link, in-app DM + 6 credits + one-click import of prompt pack + live workshop invite.
Ava Chen: Workshop: 25m total — 10m demo, 10m live build, 5m Q&A. Will feature a support summarizer and a code->explain prompt.
Ravi Singh: routing: use quickform RSVP with calendar embed. want to test delivery channel effect (email vs in-app). Need import endpoint to track prompt_imported.
Priya Nair: benchmarks/launch post will be small: 2 charts + TL;DR showing sample time-to-first-gen improvements with starter prompts. Post links to replay+pack.
growth-bot: experiment created: id=exp_startersprint_2999999999, variants=3, start=2999999999, status=scheduled
Maya Patel: draft experiment config for eng:
```
variants: [control, email_credits_replay, inapp_credits_import]
audience_filter: signup_days <= 12
metrics: trial_start_7d, prompt_imported, prompt_runs_2, time_to_first_gen
```
Jon Rivera: success criteria: >=12% uplift in trial_start_7d OR >=20% lift in prompt_imported vs control.
Ava Chen: prompt pack path: /share/promptpacks/startersprint-v1. will add README and one-click import metadata.
Ravi Singh: eng will add POST /internal/exp/import?exp=2999999999 to emit prompt_imported with variant/channel. ETA Fri.
Maya Patel: email subject variants to A/B: "Starter Sprint + 6 credits" vs "Watch a 25m workshop + try prompts (6 credits)". in-app copy shorter: "Import prompts + 6 credits".
Priya Nair: remember UTM tags: utm_source=startersprint&utm_variant={{variant}} and campaign=startersprint_launch.
Maya Patel: timeline: creative Thu, import hook Fri, smoke Sat, launch Mon AM. will collect 48h signal and a 7-day retrospective.
Jon Rivera: add a 2-question micro-survey for attendees: NPS 0–10 + "Did the prompts help you ship?" yes/no.
Ava Chen: RSVPs trickling in :tada: sharing attendee list in #growth-private.
Maya Patel: tagging @eng-ops and @growth for final sign-off. Any blockers?
Ravi Singh: none from infra; need final promptpack id by Wed.
Maya Patel: thanks all — will post results after 7 days. :rocket:

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Quick ask — we need a multiformat exec asset pack for the benchmark launch next week. Includes: email teaser, hero for social, webinar slide diagram (latency vs cost), a one-pager PDF, and 3 social motion GIFs. :sparkles:

Ava Chen: Requirements: localized copy (EN/FR/DE/JP), brand compliance check, export-ready files (SVGs for diagrams + PNG/JPEG web sizes), light/dark colorways, and accessibility alt text. Can design own mock copy if content isn't final but flag it.

Diego Rivera: Got it. Can you drop a Figma file or starter frames? Any hard constraints on illustration style or existing icon set to use?

Ava Chen: Figma starter here: https://www.figma.com/file/benchmark-xyz/benchmark-assets — I added a rough hero and a sketch of the latency-cost curve. Use the brand icon set in the "components/icons" page.

Priya Nair: I'll paste the copy for the hero + email subject lines below. Use these as primary: 
- Hero headline: "Inference that balances speed and unit cost"
- Subhead: "Ship features faster with predictable latency and lower per-token spend"
- Email subject (A): "See how we cut inference costs without sacrificing latency"
- Email subject (B): "Benchmark: throughput, latency, and the economics of inference"

Diego Rivera: Perfect. For the webinar diagram — do we want an annotated curve (points for host mode vs quantized) or a clean chart? Also: SVG text or outlines?

Ava Chen: Annotated curve preferred with 3 callouts (Hosted API, Dedicated, Private). Keep text live in SVG (not outlines) so we can localize later. Callouts should have short copy — Priya can provide.

Priya Nair: Callouts: 
- Hosted: "fastest time-to-value"
- Dedicated: "predictable throughput, isolation"
- Private: "compliance & control"

Lina Patel: Timeline & deliverables from design ops POV: 
- Initial comps (desktop + mobile hero + diagram): due in 48 hours (2987810000).
- Localization-ready files and one-pager PDF: due in 4 business days.
- Final exported assets (all sizes + motion GIFs): due EOD before launch date.

Lina Patel: Export specs I want in the handoff: 
- Social hero: 1200x628 (PNG) + 1200x628 (optimized JPG for ads)
- LinkedIn carousel tile: 1080x1080 (PNG)
- Twitter/Facebook teaser: 1200x675
- Email header: 600x200 (PNG)
- One-pager: PDF (A4 + US Letter) with embedded SVG diagrams
- Webinar slide: 1920x1080 SVG + PNG fallback
- Motion GIFs: 800x800, 3 variants (loop, 6s, 3s)

Diego Rivera: Re: motion — what's allowed for animation? subtle sparkles or movement on the curve? Any brand rules against motion?

Ava Chen: Keep motion subtle and not flashy. No strobing. Follow brand motion tokens in Figma. Tag Legal for any claims.

Grace (Legal): Quick flag — any claim referencing specific percentage improvements needs a data source callout in the asset or a footnote in the one-pager. If you want to include a sample stat, send source text to legal@redwood.com for review.

Diego Rivera: Noted. We'll avoid concrete %s unless Priya supplies a vetted stat.

Priya Nair: I can include a qualitative stat: "typical latency improvements without model changes" — if Legal needs data-backed numbers we'll surface in a footnote.

DesignBot: Automated reminder — please attach the Figma link to the Asana task and tag #brand-review when ready.

Diego Rivera: Quick question about localization: do we own translations or should I export placeholder labels in English and hand to ops?

Lina Patel: Ops will coordinate translations — please keep copy as editable text layers in the SVGs and provide an exported CSV of copy keys. Example CSV columns: key, en, fr, de, jp.

Diego Rivera: Will do. Also planning to provide both outline and live-text SVGs for diagrams so we have fallbacks.

Ava Chen: Awesome — final asks: please include a small checklist in the handoff: alt text, file naming convention (redwood_bench_<asset>_<size>_<locale>), color hexes used, and accessibility contrast check screenshot.

Lina Patel: For accessibility we need AA contrast on hero text. Attach the contrast report (screenshot) in the final handoff.

Diego Rivera: Understood. I'll post first comps in ~36 hours to this thread and ping ops for translations once text keys are exported.

Ava Chen: Thank you — major :thumbsup:. If anything blocks you ping me.

Grace (Legal): I'll do a quick pass once comps are ready. Cheers.

DesignBot: Export job queued: 2987650015 — will post exports to #asset-uploads when complete.

Ava Chen: Closing loop — thanks everyone. We'll review comps in the Friday design sync. :calendar:

source.messages:
Clara Ito: Quick sync — planning the benchmark launch nurture. Need newsletter + webinar sequence that works across NA/EU/APAC. Key goals: maximize webinar RSVPs, local attendance, and report downloads. Thinking: language-aware segmentation, 3 pre-event emails + 2 post-event followups, plus calendar ICS and timezone-aware reminders.
Mateo Alvarez: I can own segmentation & send windows. Proposed segments: en-US, en-EU (UK/IE), es-ES/LA, ja, and de. Also a separate "customer-high-touch" segment routed via AEs with private RSVP links.
Priya Shah: Copy plan: create compact variants per language and then a brevity experiment — short subject + snappy body vs longer persuasive copy. A/B test copy length within each language on engaged cohorts. For subjects: include local timezone mention when appropriate, e.g., "Live: Benchmark walkthrough — 10am BST / 2am PT".
Greg Novak: ESP notes: dynamic content blocks supported for 5 locales. For any locale not available default to English. Calendar add: include ics and Google Calendar links; watch URL length for some providers. Also ensure encoded UTF-8 subject support for non-Latin scripts.
Deliverability Bot: Deliverability checks: seed tests in localized inboxes (Gmail US, Gmail JP, GMX DE), check feedback loop rates, and monitor for character-set drop issues. Avoid long quoted-printable encoded subjects (>75 chars) which some ISPs truncate. For large sends, throttle per-region to avoid ISP soft-bans.
Clara Ito: Segmentation filters (export-ready) — keep simple:
```
segment_en_us: locale:en-US AND opt_in:newsletter AND engagement_60d:gte1
segment_ja: locale:ja AND opt_in:newsletter AND engagement_90d:gte1
segment_customer_high: tag:customer AND ae_owner:exists
```
Mateo Alvarez: Send windows (local): NA customers -> Tue 10:00 PT, EU locales -> Wed 11:00 BST, APAC locales -> Thu 09:00 JST. For broad newsletter cohorts send at local morning and schedule throttles: max_chunk=15k, pause=10m.
Priya Shah: Testing plan: within each language run a brevity A/B (Short vs Long) seeded with 10% sample, pick winner at 18h. For subjects also test "calendar-in-subject" vs no-calendar tag on high-touch customers. Track opens -> RSVP -> attendance funnel.
Greg Novak: QA checklist: localized seeds (support@, product@, 1 regional inbox per locale), verify charset in subject for JP/DE, test ICS & Google calendar links, verify personalization tokens in each locale fallback. Also validate List-Unsubscribe header and plain-text fallbacks.
Deliverability Bot: Suppress rules: downloaded_report_45d:true, unsub_product:true, hard_bounce_90d:true, spam_trap_flag:true. For locales with strict consent (DE) double-check legal opt-in fields.
Clara Ito: Ops Bot — create campaign skeleton with locale sub-campaigns and tasks: segments (Mateo), copy briefs & brevity test (Priya), deliverability QA (Greg). Set dry-run 6 days before primary send.
Ops Bot: Campaign draft created: "Global Benchmark Launch — Multi-locale Newsletter + Webinar Nurture". Tasks added: segmentation (Mateo), copy & tests (Priya), deliverability QA (Greg). Dry-run scheduled 6 days before send.
Mateo Alvarez: Note on metrics: report downloads tracked via utm_campaign=bench_global_launch, utm_medium=email, utm_source=newsletter, utm_content={{locale}}_{{variant}}. For AE links include ae_owner param.
Priya Shah: I'll drop short-long copy variants for en/ja/de/es in the doc today. Keep images lightweight for locales with higher mobile rates.
Greg Novak: Final ops note: run localized seed spam check 48h prior. If any locale seed spam rate >0.35% hold that locale. Also schedule staggered sends by region to smooth SMTP load.
Clara Ito: Perfect — small, focused sends per locale, brevity test, calendar links, and AE private invites. Thanks everyone, dropping initial copy in doc by EOD. :earth_americas:

source.messages:
Rae Chen: Heads-up: want to run a compact 'Welcome & Webinar' nurture for new newsletter signups next week. Primary goal: drive webinar RSVPs and surface preference signals. Keep it lightweight (2 emails). Thoughts? :sparkles:

Omar Ali: Good idea. Recommend segment by signup intent tag (doc_downloaded, demo_requested, general). That will give cleaner signal on which creative works.

Priya Natarajan: Copy plan: Email 1 = welcome + single CTA to RSVP (highlight one benchmark stat). Email 2 (48h later for opens only) = quick how-to + reminder. Two subject variants for Email 1: "Welcome + Benchmarks" vs "Start in 5 minutes". A/B 50/50 within each intent segment.

Cam (Analytics): Measurement: primary KPI = RSVP rate @ D7, uplift vs control. Please add utm_campaign=news-welcome-2667, intent_tag, subject_var. Also keep a small 5% control that gets a plain text note.

Devon (Ops): Throttle plan: seeds first, then 40/min for first hour then ramp. Add jitter +/-15m. If hard_bounce > 1% pause.

Rae Chen: Draft handshake body (short): "Hey {{first_name}} — welcome! See a quick snapshot of our latest inference benchmarks and join a 20-min live Q&A. RSVP -> [link]. Prefer product tips? Click here to switch." Keep <120 words.

Omar Ali: For the RSVP landing page, prefill email + intent_tag and add a hidden field for lead_score. Also tag RSVP events for downstream scoring.

Priya Natarajan: I'll prepare the two subject variants and the follow-up micro-email tonight. Also add an alternate CTA for 'Snooze' to reduce complaints.

Cam (Analytics): I'll create the dashboard filters and the uplift calc. Null hypothesis: subject A drives >=1.2ppt higher RSVP than B. We'll run test for 7 days.

Devon (Ops): I'll run seed sends Tue 10am, create cohorts Wed, and enable jitter on Fri send. Posting send script in #eng-ops when ready.

Rae Chen: Action items:
- Priya: deliver subject variants + follow-up copy
- Devon: cohorts, seeds, throttle + jitter
- Cam: dashboard + tagging spec
- Omar: landing prefill + RSVP event hook

Rae Chen: Can QA be tomorrow EOD? If green, schedule sends for Fri 11:00 PST. Thanks team! :raised_hands:

source.messages:
Maya Rivera: Quick idea — want to run a 'warm pulse' weekend send for trial signups this Friday. Short handshake email + 3-day micro-nurture. Goal: convert high-intent -> webinar RSVP, others -> standard nurture. Thoughts on staggering windows to avoid queue spikes? :wave:

Sam Chen: Love it. My rec is 3 cohorts by activity signals: (A) product tour / API used (high intent), (B) signed up but no usage (warm), (C) partner/marketing signups (cold). Stagger sends by cohort to smooth throughput and give each cohort time to react. :+1:

Sam Chen: Proposed windows (rough):
```
Cohort A: Fri 11:00-11:30 PST
Cohort B: Fri 12:00-13:00 PST
Cohort C: Sat 09:00-10:30 PST
```

Priya Natarajan: For subject lines / CTA split: test urgency vs. value. Two quick variants: 1) "Welcome — quick setup guide + live office hours" 2) "Get the most from your trial in 5 minutes". A/B with RSVP CTA vs. product-setup CTA. Keep body <= 120 words.

Elena Gomez: We'll need tracking tags: utm_campaign=warm-pulse-2612, cohort= A|B|C, email_variant=A|B. Add an event for "handshake_rsvp" and "micro_nurture_open" so growth can measure lift. Also plan a fallback path if SES bounce rate spikes.

Ash (Growth): Suggest we add a tiny randomized delay per message (0-45m) within the scheduled window to avoid batch bursts — basically a Poisson-ish spread. Low overhead, big smoothing benefit. Also think about prioritizing webhook/eng routes for Cohort A so they get lower latency landing experiences.

Maya Rivera: Draft handshake copy (short):
"Hey {{first_name}} — welcome! We noticed you started a trial. Join a 20-min walkthrough this week to get live tips and a free prompt pack. RSVP -> [link]. If you prefer, try our 2-minute quickstart guide"

Sam Chen: Love that. For the micro-nurture cadence: Day 0 handshake, Day 1 quick tip, Day 3 use-case story + invite, Day 7 re-engage with case study. Keep each email focused on one CTA.

Priya Natarajan: One more ask — can marketing ops actually create the segments today so we can QA on Wed? If yes, I'll prepare the 2 subject variants and schedule the test cell (~10% each) and control for measurement.

Elena Gomez: Ops can create segments today. I'll add the tracking and set alarm for delivery failure >1% and bounce >2%.

Ash (Growth): I'll own the KPI doc: open, click, RSVP rate, conversion to active usage at D7. Will drop a short dashboard link here when ready.

Maya Rivera: Perfect. Action items:
- Ops: create segments + tracking (Elena)
- Copy: Priya to deliver two subject variants + 3 micro-emails
- Send cadence + throttling: Sam to finalize windows and jitter setup
- KPIs: Ash to create dashboard

Maya Rivera: If we can QA by Thu EOD and approve, schedule for Fri morning batch for Cohort A. Will we route webinar invites to a special landing page? (prefer yes)

Elena Gomez: Yes — route to webinar landing with prefilled params. Will confirm page capacity.

Sam Chen: OK — I'll post final windows + the jitter script in #eng-ops once QA passes. :rocket:

Maya Rivera: Thanks all — excited to see if a tight weekend pulse moves trial conversion. :tada:

source.content:
Maya Chen: Quick sync - planning a small PLG pilot: combine a live webinar (Tue 9am PT), 50 free credits for attendees, and a 3-template prompt pack. Goal: improve 7-day activation and first-API-call conversion. Need inbound routing rules + the activation nudge cadence. Who can own which piece?
Ethan Park: I can own the cohort experiment setup and tracking. Hypothesis: credits + tailored prompt templates => faster first-sent request and higher retention. Want A: credits only, B: credits + prompt pack, C: credits + prompt pack + email nudge.
Priya Singh: I’ll design the landing and prompt pack. Quick Q - do we want a single signup flow or separate CTAs per cohort? My vote: single signup, hidden cohort flag so we don't confuse users.
Noah Rivera: I'll wire up dashboards. Need the exact event names and properties. Please standardize on event: "first_api_call" and property: "webinar_cohort" (values A/B/C). Also want "credit_grant" event.
Zoe Patel: Routing ops: we'll push leads to HubSpot + Intercom. I can add a lead property "webinar_cohort" and a workflow to tag and route to the right onboarding playbook. Can we confirm the mapping for source -> cohort?
Maya Chen: Mapping plan:
source -> cohort
organic_webinar_landing -> A (credits only)
promo_webinar_link_A -> B (credits + prompts)
promo_webinar_link_B -> C (full with nudge)
We will use UTM param utm_cohort=A/B/C on the registration links. Zoe - can HubSpot ingest utm_cohort automatically?
Zoe Patel: Yes - HubSpot form captures utm params. I'll create a workflow: if utm_cohort == B then set webinar_cohort=B and add tag "promptpack-B". Also add Slack alert to #growth when a C cohort lead arrives.
Priya Singh: Draft copy for the thank-you email and the one-click prompt pack download. Feedback welcome:
Subject: Thanks for signing up - here are your 50 credits + prompt pack
Hey {{firstName}},
Thanks for joining the webinar. Your account has been credited with 50 free tokens - use them to try these starter prompts:
1) Product Idea Generator - "Help me design a 3-step user flow for a writing assistant for X."
2) Sales Email Personalizer - "Rewrite this outreach email for role Y at company Z."
3) Onboarding Bot Script - "Create an onboarding checklist for a developer integrating an API."
Click here to enable the prompts in your dashboard: <link with utm_cohort={{utm_cohort}}>
- The Redwood Team
Ethan Park: Love it. For the activation nudge cadence (C cohort): 0h credits + prompts; 24h: quick tip email; 48h: short in-app prompt suggestion; 6 days: friendly reminder with example use case. Noah - please add these time-based triggers to the experiment metrics.
Noah Rivera: Will do. I will create cohort segments and funnel: signup -> first_api_call (within 7 days) -> second_api_call (within 14 days). Primary metric: pct(first_api_call | signup). Secondary: pct(second_api_call). Also add cost per activated user (credits spent).
Maya Chen: Ops checklist before Tuesday:
- [ ] Priya: final landing + prompt pack assets (deadline Fri EOD)
- [ ] Zoe: HubSpot form + workflows + Slack alert (Mon AM)
- [ ] Ethan: experiment config + variant links (Mon AM)
- [ ] Noah: dashboards + event schema + alerts (Mon PM)
- [ ] Growth: set webinar registration CTA and paid promo (Mon PM)
Any blockers?
Priya Singh: No blockers on design, just need final copy tweaks from Ethan.
Ethan Park: I'll send copy edits by EOD today. Also can we shorten the CTA path for C cohort - single click to enable prompts? Priya - doable?
Priya Singh: Yep - will add one-click enable token to the thank-you page.
Zoe Patel: One more - to avoid noise, only send Slack alert for cohort C if company_email != gmail/yahoo (sales leads). Otherwise route to general growth queue.
Maya Chen: Good call. Noah - can you include that filter in the tracking?
Noah Rivera: Yup - add property company_email_domain and filter in the cohort C alert rule.
onboarding-bot: Reminder: Webinar event on Tue 09:00 PT. Add final attendee list to #events by Mon 10:00 PT. :calendar:
Maya Chen: Thanks all - let's reconvene Mon 11am PT for final preflight. :thumbsup:

Leah Park: Final checklist for Tuesday's Virtual Summit 10am PT: reg page, Zoom settings, recording export, HubSpot routing. Reg page: https://redwood.typeform.com/vsummit_signup?utm_campaign=vsummit_jun26
Sam Ortiz: I'm on Typeform -> will add field session_interest and hidden utm_campaign. Copy updated in doc: https://docs.google.com/d/VS_copy
Rina Kapoor: Zoom settings to confirm before start:

- cloud_recording: enabled (speaker + gallery + screen)
- auto_transcribe: enabled (SRT)
- registration: none (we use Typeform)

- allow_multiple_hosts: true

Leah Park: Consent language needs to be explicit on Typeform: "I consent to being recorded and for my likeness/voice to be used in marketing materials." Confirmed?
Sam Ortiz: Consent checkbox added. Also mapping Typeform -> HubSpot: email, first_name, utm_campaign, session_interest. Webhook endpoint: /webhooks/hubspot/vsummit (tested).

Asha Patel: Quick sync: CloudArc wants a co-marketing push: marketplace listing, a technical 40-min webinar, and a short benchmark blog. They want marketplace live the same week as the blog. Can we outline owners + inbound routing?
Ben Carter: Target audience = platform engineers and infra leads. Benchmarks should show p95 and cost per 100k tokens vs their prior managed endpoint.
Lina Morales: I can run the webinar content and host the demo. Need CloudArc co-host and a customer if possible.
partnership-bot: Draft event slot: "CloudArc + Redwood: Operationalizing low-latency LLM inference" 2025-10-07 10:00 PT. Calendar link: https://cal.example.com/e/xyz789
Asha Patel: Proposed timeline: content freeze 9/26, rehearsal 10/03, webinar 10/07, blog + listing 10/09. Owners: Webinar/host: Lina, Benchmarks: Ben, Blog/listing copy + tracking: Asha.
Ben Carter: Routing matrix (CSV):
source,utm_campaign,initial_owner,followup_queue
webinar,cloudarc-web-1007,SDR-webinar,SDR-inbound
blog,cloudarc-blog-1009,Content-SDR,SDR-inbound
marketplace,cloudarc-listing-1009,AE-partner,AE-triage
Lina Morales: Messaging A/B: short headline for listing vs long-form blog headline. CTA uniform: "Request architecture review" to standardize lead flow.
Asha Patel: Legal/brand: need CloudArc logo + co-brand approval by 9/30. Ben, can you request assets?
Ben Carter: Will ping CloudArc PM now. Also will anonymize benchmark tables (model, seq len, batch) and publish by 9/22.
partnership-bot: Reminder set: checklist review 2025-09-24 09:00 PT.
Asha Patel: Action recap: Lina - slides + runbook (due 10/03). Ben - benchmarks + logo request (benchmarks due 9/22). Asha - listing copy + UTM params (9/29). If no objections Ill create the Notion hub and tag owners.
Lina Morales: +1
Ben Carter: +1
Asha Patel: Thanks all, Ill post the Notion link once ready and loop CloudArc for marketplace specs. :rocket:

source.messages:
Leah Kim: quick idea  run a compact launch tying a short benchmark post + 30m miniworkshop + 8 credits to an inbound routing test. goal: lift prompt adoption and trial starts at 7d.
Maya Patel: sample? arms?
Leah Kim: 4k recent signups (0-10d). three arms: control, email benchmark+8 credits, in-app nudge + one-click import + replay.
Jon Rivera: benchmark narrative: 3 quick charts (latency, cost, time-to-first-gen) + 2-line TL;DR. use across launch post, email, in-app.
Ava Chen: workshop 30m: 12m demo (6 starter prompts), 8m live build, 10m Q&A. we'll record and gate replay behind quickform.
Ravi Singh: routing: lightweight quickform for RSVP, calendar embed. variant delivery channel must be fixed per user. eng ops needs config.
Leah Kim: metrics: primary=trial_conv_7d. secondaries=prompt_imported, prompt_runs>=2, time_to_first_gen, replay_rsvp, attendee_nps.
analytics-bot: experiment created id=exp_ignite_2165432101 variants=3 start=2165432101 status=scheduled
Maya Patel: copy sketches: email subject A: "Benchmark: 3x faster to useful outputs (+8 credits)" subject B: "Try our starter prompts  8 free credits". in-app: short CTA "Import prompts + 8 credits".
Jon Rivera: add UTM: utm_campaign=ignite_launch&utm_variant={{variant}}. track RSVP->attended and import->first-gen attribution.
eng-ops: will expose POST /internal/exp/import?exp=2165432101 to emit prompt_imported with variant/channel meta. ETA Fri EOD for hooks.
Leah Kim: success criteria: >=15% rel uplift trial_conv_7d OR >=25% lift prompt_imported vs control.
Ava Chen: uploading promptpack to /share/promptpacks/ignite-v1 now. include code->doc and product-description prompts.
Ravi Singh: include opt-out in emails and replay privacy note. also ensure replay modal shows import button that calls import endpoint.
Maya Patel: ops checklist: creative, calendar+quickform, import endpoint, email/in-app templates, GA/segment mapping, reporting dashboard. timeline: creative Wed, hooks Fri, launch Mon.
Leah Kim: will run initial 48h check and a 7-day retrospective. tagging @eng-ops @growth for final sign-off. any blockers?

source.thread:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — numbers from the weekend launch posts + credits funnel are in. We need decisions on webinar CTA, inbound routing, and whether to push the new onboarding prompt templates. TL;DR: CTR good, activation not great.

Mateo Ruiz: Shared dashboard earlier — CTR 8.4% (post -> signup), but of signups only 18% hit the “activate” step. High drop at onboarding screen

Priya Singh: Thoughts: two quick experiments: 1) swap webinar CTA to “Get 50 free prompt tokens” vs “Join webinar” 2) route webinar signups with >company_size=50 to sales pipeline. Both low lift

Jules Park: For benchmark narrative we should call out “time-to-first-quality-response” — that's what enterprise cares about. Also can we A/B hero copy to mention “templates for dev onboarding”

Sofia Li: I'd hold prompt-template rollout until the variant 2 feels stable. We risk confusing users if templates change mid-flow. :thinking_face:

GrowthBot: Experiment summary (24h window):
```
variant_A (Join webinar CTA): 12,184 impressions -> 1,020 clicks (8.37%) -> 189 signups -> 34 activations
variant_B (50 tokens CTA): 11,902 impressions -> 1,032 clicks (8.67%) -> 242 signups -> 63 activations
lift in activation rate: +3.8pp (stat sig p=0.02)
```

Mateo Ruiz: Variant B wins for activation. Recommend: switch main post CTA to tokens, add routing rule for enterprise leads, and schedule canary of template rollout to 5% of new signups

Ava Chen: Agree. Action items:
- Mateo: change live post CTA & update paid ads creative (today)
- Priya: add GCLID-based routing rule for company_size >50 to sales (this week)
- Jules: prepare new hero copy + benchmark blurb for release notes (draft by tomorrow)
- Sofia: hold template rollout to canary 5%, monitor TTFQ metric

Priya Singh: Also recommend quick in-app nudge at onboarding (copy below). Low dev cost.
```
Nudge copy v1: "Start with a prompt template — try 'One-line PR summary' with your repo in 30s. Get 50 free tokens."
```

Jules Park: +1 on nudge. Add tracking event: onboarding_nudge_shown, onboarding_template_used

Ava Chen: Thanks team. We'll regroup Tuesday AM. :rocket:

Mira Patel: Quick: partners-only briefing scheduled for T-30 (30m before public launch). We need a short partner one-pager and demo link. Can PR ops handle distribution?
Jon Park: I can send the partner package at T-30 via the partner portal. Package = one-pager, embargoed blog PDF, short demo video. Need partner list confirm by T-45.
Ava Chen: Legal note: partners must agree to hold until public T0. Use the phrase "observed in our internal tests" in all stats. No guarantees.
Rohan Das: Design will output a partner-branded PDF and a 60s demo clip by T-40. Uploading to /shared/partners in 20m.
Mira Patel: Distribution plan: T-30 push to portal + email digest to partner leads. Ask them to not share publicly. Also add a short FAQ addressing reseller/customer questions.
Jon Park: Confirmed. I'll include a feedback form link so partners can send questions; I'll route responses to PR and product.
commops-bot: Scheduled task created: partner-package:deploy -> 08:30 PT (T-30). Will post completion to #marketing and DM @Mira Patel.
legal-bot: Reminder: Ensure the NDA checkbox is recorded for each partner before package access. Automate retraction if checkbox not present.
Mira Patel: Great. Contingency: if partner leaks, escalate to legal + PR, and issue clarifying note to partners and press. Everyone standby T-45 to T+15.
Ava Chen: Thanks team. I'll join the partner call at T-28 to intro the briefing. :handshake:

source.messages:
Maya Patel: Quick kickoff — proposal: pair a 30m "Quickstart Workshop" + starter prompt pack + a small credit incentive to push first-generation and 7d trial starts. Goal: measurable lift in first-run completion.
Jon Rivera: Sounds good. What's the sample and the arms?
Maya Patel: ~5k recent signups (0–14d). Three arms: control, email invite + 8 credits, in-app DM + 8 credits + single-click promptpack import + replay access.
Ava Chen: Workshop plan: 30m total — 12m demo of 6 starter prompts, 8m live build, 10m Q&A. We’ll show a code->doc and a customer-support summarizer.
Ravi Singh: Routing: prefer a lightweight RSVP quickform (calendar embed) to avoid landing page drag. Need channel split (email vs in-app) to test delivery impact.
Maya Patel: Metrics: primary = trial_start_7d. Secondary = prompt_imported, prompt_runs>=2, time_to_first_gen, replay_views, attendee_nps.
eng-ops: We can add a /internal/exp/import_pack endpoint that emits prompt_imported with variant and channel metadata. ETA Fri EOD for hooks.
Maya Patel: Draft experiment config (for eng ops):
```
exp_id: exp_quickstart_2102468000
variants: [control, email_credits, inapp_credits_import]
audience_filter: signup_days <= 14
metrics: trial_start_7d, prompt_imported, prompt_runs_2, time_to_first_gen, replay_rsvp
```
Jon Rivera: Success criteria: >=12% relative uplift in trial_start_7d OR >=20% increase in prompt_imported vs control.
Ava Chen: Promptpack is being built in /share/promptpacks/quickstart-v4. Will include a short onboarding doc and one-click import metadata.
Maya Patel: Email subject A/B ideas: "Quickstart Workshop + 8 free credits" vs "Try these starter prompts — 8 credits inside". In-app copy should be shorter and action-focused.
Ravi Singh: Add UTM query params utm_source=quickstart&utm_variant={{variant}}. Also ensure RSVP->attended and import->first_gen attribution in events.
growth-bot: experiment logged: id=exp_quickstart_2102468000, variants=3, start=2102468000, status=scheduled
Maya Patel: Timeline: creative by Thu, import hook & telemetry by Fri, smoke test Sat, launch Mon AM. Tagging @eng-ops and @growth for final sign-off.
Jon Rivera: Include a 2-question post-event micro-survey: NPS + Did these prompts help you ship something? (yes/no).
eng-ops: Noted. We’ll instrument prompt_imported, first_generation_time, replay_rsvp, and trial_start event with variant/channel. Need final promptpack id by Thu EOD.
Ava Chen: RSVPs already trickling in — sharing attendee list in #growth-private.
Maya Patel: Great — I'll post interim 48h signal and a 7-day retrospective with benchmarks and next steps. :rocket:

source.messages:
Olivia Park: Quick sync on launch newsletter + 7-day drip for webinar no-shows. Goal: convert product watchers into trial users. Need segment rules, cadence, and warmup plan.
Sanjeev Rao: Agree. We should split no-shows by engagement: watched >30s vs landed only. Different subject/tone.
Tess Morgan: Copy idea for watched >30s: 'You missed the live demo — clips & benchmarks inside' For landed-only: 'See what you missed: 3-minute benchmark highlights'
Priya Natarajan: Segment sizes: watched>30s = 1.8k, landed-only = 3.4k, registrants who converted = 600 (suppress). Also compile a control holdback of 5% per segment for lift measurement.
Micah Lee: Drip cadence proposal: Day0 (send recap & clips), Day2 (short benchmark email + CTA to trial), Day5 (customer story + limited time credit). For no-shows, use lower frequency.
Olivia Park: Deliverability: we need to warm up by sending small batches to high-engagement users first. Plan: send 5% to top-engaged, wait 15m, then 20%, then remainder over 2 hours. Ops rate limit 900/min.
Sanjeev Rao: Also set seed list to major ISPs and internal inboxes to check rendering and spam traps. Alert if bounce rate >2% in first 30m.
Tess Morgan: A/B test subject + CTA placement. Test subject (A): 'Benchmark: 2x latency, 3x cost savings'. (B): 'Redwood 2026: faster inference for production' CTA test: hero CTA vs inline CTA.
Priya Natarajan: UTM params: utm_campaign=launch_march2026_drip, utm_medium=email, utm_source=newsletter. Add campaign tags to CRM so ops can attribute conversions.
Olivia Park: Suppress list: customers with active contract, unsubscribed, bounced last 90d, and people already in paid-trial funnel.
BrandBot: New campaign draft created: https://app.redwood/marketing/campaigns/2024501122 (id 8941) :white_check_mark:
Micah Lee: Sample campaign config for ops (paste into campaign manager):
```
name: "launch_march2026_drip"
segments:
  - webinar_no_show_watched
  - webinar_no_show_landed
exclude:
  - registrants_converted
  - active_customers
holdback: "5%"
send_strategy:
  - stage: "seeding"
    percents: [5,20,75]
    pause_minutes: 15
  - stage: "remainder"
    window_minutes: 120
ab_tests:
  - subject: ["A","B"]
  - cta_position: ["hero","inline"]
utm: "utm_campaign=launch_march2026_drip&utm_source=newsletter"
ops_alerts:
  - bounce_rate_threshold: "2%"
  - deliverability_drop: "15%"
```
Olivia Park: Nice. Last call for copy by 14:00 PT. I'll approve final send plan at 16:00 PT if no red flags. :eyes:

source.thread:
Ava Chen: Heads-up: final social queue for next week's 'Model Serving Playbook' launch. Need X + LinkedIn + webinar routing. TL;DR: 2 X deliverables (teaser + benchmark thread), 3 LI posts, webinar CTA everywhere. Need creatives by Fri EOD. Assign owners.

Liam O'Connor: Added webinar calendar on Tue 11am PT. @Olivia can you confirm schedule grid? Also need UTM pattern and SDR routing.

Olivia Rodriguez: On it. I'll handle scheduling + UTM. Confirming timezones: Tue 11am PT = Tue 19:00 UTC. Will set auto-posts in Buffer and add tracking. Need final copy + assets by Fri 5pm PT.

Noah Patel: Uploading assets to Notion: hero.jpg, hero-2.jpg, quote-card-1.png, quote-card-2.png. Link: https://notion.redwood/serving-playbook/assets (placeholder). Alt text included in the uploads.

Ava Chen: Dropping copy drafts here:
```
X teaser (single tweet):
We built a practical playbook for serving LLMs in production — latency, cost, and reliability are where product wins happen. Join our free webinar to see benchmarks + configs. rwd.io/playbook?utm_campaign=serving_launch

Benchmark thread (5 tweets):
1/ Serving LLMs at scale isn’t just model choice. It’s batching, caching, routing. Here’s a 90s breakdown + our benchmark highlights.
2/ Optimized runtime -> 30% lower p99 latency vs baseline on 8k context.
3/ Intelligent KV caching -> 2x fewer tokens billed for common prefixes.
4/ Routing + fallbacks kept SLAs during a regional outage in our test.
5/ Join the webinar Tue 11am PT for the full methodology + scripts. rwd.io/playbook?utm_campaign=serving_launch

LinkedIn longform (draft):
We shipped a Model Serving Playbook that captures the lessons from running production LLM inference at scale. In the webinar we'll share end-to-end benchmarks, practical configs (batching, quantization), and playbook next steps for platform teams. RSVP: rwd.io/playbook
```

Liam O'Connor: Looks good. Shorten CTA to rwd.io/playbook (nice). Add utm_campaign=serving_launch. Can we A/B headlines on LI?

Olivia Rodriguez: Yes — scheduling A/B with two headlines. For routing: high-intent (webinar signups + company email) -> SDR queue 'incoming-webinar-joins' ; others -> nurture list. @Liam can you set webhook to HubSpot form?

Liam O'Connor: Webhook done. Form field 'interest_level' added. SDR owner: Maya (sales). I'll forward the webhook test log shortly.

engage-bot: Auto-posts scheduled: 2 X posts, 3 LinkedIn posts. First goes live Tue 10:00 PT. Tracking enabled (utm_campaign=serving_launch).

Ava Chen: :rocket: thanks team. Quick checklist: final copy done? assets uploaded? webinar link checked? shortlinks ready?

Noah Patel: Copy locked. Assets uploaded and mobile QA passed. Minor alt-text tweak done.

Olivia Rodriguez: All set on scheduling. I'll post confirmation in #launch-daily Tue morning and monitor engagement. Follow-ups: first 24h high-intent -> Maya; social replies -> community; threaded replies needing quote cards -> @Noah.

Liam O'Connor: Putting a 1-hour standby on Tue 11-12 PT for live ops. @Maya will get lead batch every 30 min.

Ava Chen: Perfect. I'll do a final check-in Tue 9am PT. :eyes:

source.messages:
Nora Alvarez: Quick thread 1: need a tight benchmark narrative for launch: hero stat + two charts + routing plan. Deadline: next Tue.
Ben Carter: I can do charts. Propose: Chart 1 = p95 latency vs $/1k tokens across 4 model variants. Chart 2 = session-level cost + roundtrip latency for a 2-turn chat.
Ivy Zhou: Messaging: hero stat = "sub-100ms p95 on typical chat workloads" but back it with cost tradeoff in subhead. Also add a short methodology note.
Ben Carter: Mock CSV Is to use: model,mode,p95_ms,cost_per_1k
redwood-8k,quantized,85,0.09
open-7b,hosted,140,0.22
open-1.3b,hosted,70,0.05
DesignBot: Reminder: use Redwood palette (red #D8433B, slate #263238, warm-gray #9E9E9E). Deliver PNG + SVG.
Nora Alvarez: Routing plan: webinar signups -> nurture sequence + whitepaper; PLG signups -> credits + docs; enterprise -> sales outreach if company domain detected. Add utm_campaign=bench_march26.
Ivy Zhou: Draft hero lines: "Keep production chat snappy: predictable tail latency at scale." Subhead: "How routing, batching, and quantization trade off latency and cost in real apps."
Ben Carter: I'll upload two visual drafts by Fri EOD. Can we review Mon morning?
Nora Alvarez: Yes. I'll book 45min review and share invite. Ping here with links when ready. :rocket:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — want to run a small PLG experiment next week that combines a 15-min microwebinar + staggered free credits to boost onboarding completion. Thoughts?
Samir Patel: love the idea. what's the hypothesis? more attendees convert vs just credits?
Ava Chen: hypothesis: webinar drives intent signal so we can surface higher-value prompt templates in onboarding -> higher DAU in week 1. credits act as friction remover.
Maya Lopez: can we keep the cohort small (500) and split into 3 arms: webinar+credits, credits-only, control? run for 2 weeks.
Ethan Brooks: sounds good. need routing plan for inbound leads — which signal triggers webinar invite vs immediate credits?
Samir Patel: propose rule: if sign-up via webinar ad or utm=webinar -> enroll in webinar arm; organic signups -> credits-only unless they click webinar CTA.
Maya Lopez: also add traffic control: cap webinar invites to 300 per week; overflow goes to waitlist + immediate small credit ($5)
Ava Chen: can product support templated prompt pack push during onboarding? ideally show 3 curated templates after account creation for webinar cohort.
Ethan Brooks: yes — onboarding flow has a feature flag. I'll add a flag `exp/webinar_prompt_pack_v1` and a rollout plan.
Samir Patel: draft experiment config below — please react with :thumbsup: if we can run it.
```yaml
experiment_id: onboarding_webinar_credit_stack_1943
arams:
  cohort_size: 500
  arms:
    - name: webinar_plus_credits
      promo_credit_usd: 20
      webinar_invite: true
    - name: credits_only
      promo_credit_usd: 20
      webinar_invite: false
    - name: control
      promo_credit_usd: 0
      webinar_invite: false
metrics:
  - week1_active_users
  - pct_prompt_template_use
  - avg_spend_30d
traffic_rules:
  - rule: utm_source==webinar -> assign webinar_plus_credits
  - rule: organic_with_webinar_cta -> assign webinar_plus_credits
  - else -> random_assign(credits_only, control)
```
Maya Lopez: small ask — include a soft-enrollment email sequence for webinar arm: reminder at -24h, -1h, and replay link.
Ava Chen: yep add replay tracking as a metric.
Ethan Brooks: deploy-bot: can you create the experiment flag and a canary rollout for 50 users first?
deploy-bot: created flag exp/webinar_prompt_pack_v1 at canary=50, tag=marketing/experiment/1943216789 :white_check_mark:
Samir Patel: timeline? we aim to launch next Wed -> need creative + webinar landing by Tue EOD.
Maya Lopez: I'll own landing and emails. Samir can you own tracking and analytics/events?
Samir Patel: on it. will add event names: webinar_invited, webinar_attended, prompt_template_used, credit_redeemed.
Ava Chen: perfect. final checklist: landing (Maya), emails (Maya), flag+onboarding push (Ethan), analytics (Samir), credits ops (Finance ops to ready credits).
Maya Lopez: tagging @finance-ops to prep credits.
Ava Chen: thanks all — if no objections I'll schedule a quick 15-min runbook tomorrow AM. :rocket:

source.messages:
Tara Singh: Ok team, VeloxAI signed the release form for a short customer story. Highlights: 4x median latency improvement and ~20% infra cost reduction after moving key models to Redwood Dedicated. Need interview, legal approval, design assets, and lead routing.

Ethan Park: Love it. Who's the main contact and are they ok on-record?

Tara Singh: Priya Menon, VP ML at VeloxAI. She agreed to a 20-30 min on-record interview and a 1-2 sentence quote. Also happy to provide a logo and headshot.

Lina Gomez: I can run the interview and capture a short 60s clip for socials. Tue/Thu mornings work best.

Owen Reed: I'll pull perf charts and a short metric table. Will redact any dashboard IDs. ETA Monday EOD.

Tara Singh: Key narrative: 4x P50, 20% cost reduction through batching + KV cache, more predictable tail latency. Avoid mentioning prior vendors or exact $ figures.

Ethan Park: Draft hero lines for A/B test: A) "VeloxAI sped up inference 4x with Redwood Dedicated." B) "Predictable low-latency at scale: VeloxAI's migration." Thoughts?

Lina Gomez: B for public page, A for developer channels.

Tara Singh: Also add a form checkbox: "Interested in Dedicated capacity" and a dropdown for workload type. @lead-router-bot to route trial leads to trials@redwood.ai and enterprise leads to enterprise-sales@redwood.ai, region=emea.

lead-router-bot: Routing confirmed. Trials -> trials@redwood.ai, Enterprise -> enterprise-sales@redwood.ai. Tagging rule source=case-velox, region=emea. Gated appendix mapped to enterprise-sales.

Owen Reed: Uploaded redacted graphs to Drive and added alt text. Small table:
```
Metric | Before | After
P50 latency | 120ms | 30ms
P95 latency | 480ms | 110ms
Infra cost | baseline | -20% (batching + cache)
```

Tara Singh: Perfect. Benign phrasing on cost line as above.

Ethan Park: Sent initial quote to Priya: "Redwood helped us deliver predictable latency and freed engineering cycles for model work."

Priya Menon (VeloxAI): Minor edit: change "freed engineering cycles" -> "let our engineers focus on models". Good with header + a slightly longer body quote.

Tara Singh: Noted. I'll update copy. Sending assets to legal for review (quote + images). @legal-bot please advise on logo usage.

legal-bot: Received. Please redact any customer IDs in screenshots and submit logo release (email ok). Avoid comparative claims naming competitors. Typical turnaround 48 hours.

Lina Gomez: Priya sent logo and a short release email. Uploading now.

design-bot: Noted. Will prepare a hero image, testimonial block, and social cutdowns once final copy is confirmed. Expect initial mock by Wed.

Owen Reed: Also created a technical appendix for devs with batch sizes and typical sequence lengths (no PII). Will link from the case page.

Tara Singh: Proposed timeline:
- Mon: final assets in Drive
- Wed: design QA and legal clearance
- Tue next week: publish case study + social drip
- Wed next week: webinar (if Velox wants)

Priya Menon (VeloxAI): Happy to join a webinar after publish. Also ok with the final quote: "Redwood helped us deliver predictable latency and let our engineers focus on models."

source.messages:
Mira Patel: Quick kickoff  NewtonAI signed off on a short customer story. Results: 4.2x P50 improvement and ~28% infra savings on Redwood Dedicated. Need interview, legal review, design, and lead routing.

Sam Huang: Who's the contact and quote?

Mira Patel: Anika Rao, VP Eng. She agreed to a 20-min on-record interview and a 1-2 sentence quote.

Dana Kim: I can run the interview. Thu morning works.

Leo Ortiz: I'll export perf graphs and a short table by EOD Tue.

Ben Carter: I'll draft hero copy and two social variants (dev + exec). Send technical highlights I should call out.

Mira Patel: Key points: 4.2x P50, ~28% cost after batching + KV cache, moved critical services to Dedicated for predictable tail latency. Avoid dollar figures and naming prior vendors.

Sam Huang: @Elena please route inbound leads to NA SDR queue after publish. Add tag source:case-newton.

Elena Ve1zquez: Routing set. trials -> trials@redwood.ai, enterprise -> enterprise-sales@redwood.ai. Will add checkbox: "Interested in Dedicated (predictable latency)" and map to SDR playbook.

Dana Kim: Draft interview Qs: 1) baseline infra issues 2) migration timeline and challenges 3) ops wins 4) developer velocity impact 5) one-line for header.

Mira Patel: Perfect. Ben, please prepare header and a slightly longer pull-quote for the case body.

legal-bot: Please submit final quote text and redacted screenshots for review. Avoid comparative claims naming competitors. Typical turnaround 48 hours.

Anika Rao (NewtonAI) [via Sam]: "Redwood helped us stabilize latency and accelerate delivery cycles." She OK'd a short header quote.

Ben Carter: Drafts saved to Drive: Header: "Redwood stabilized latency and sped our delivery." Pull: "Redwood helped us stabilize tail latency and let our engineers deliver features faster without firefighting infra."

Leo Ortiz: Uploaded redacted graphs and table: 
Metric | Before | After
P50 latency | 110ms | 26ms
P95 latency | 520ms | 120ms
Cost per 1k reqs | baseline | -28% (batching + cache)

Mira Patel: Keep cost phrasing high-level as above.

legal-bot: Assets received. Approved after minor redactions. Approval ref LEG-2025-599.

ops-bot: Campaign created: campaign=newton-q2, publish_window=2025-07-10_to_2025-07-17, assets=case_study,webinar. UTM tagging queued.

Elena Ve1zquez: Lead form fields updated: workload type, estimated monthly spend (optional). Mapping to NA SDR done.

Ben Carter: Scheduling social and landing copy. Two hero variants queued for A/B.

Mira Patel: Proposed timeline: 
- Tue 2025-07-01: final assets in Drive
- Thu 2025-07-03: design QA + legal clearance confirmed
- Thu 2025-07-10: publish case study + social drip
- Wed 2025-07-16: webinar with Anika (guest)

ops-bot: Publish scheduled. Live URL (once published) will be https://redwood.ai/case/newtonai.

Mira Patel: Closing thread after live URL posted. Thanks everyone! :tada:

source.messages:
Mira Patel: Hey team — want to kick off a customer story for AeroMesh. They just finished a 6-week pilot and saw a 4x reduction in per-request latency and ~30% cost savings on our Dedicated tier. Can we lock down interviews and a publish plan?

Sam Huang: Awesome. Who's the main contact at AeroMesh?

Mira Patel: Priya Rao (CTO). She's okay with a short case study + 30-minute on-record interview. She offered a 2-3 sentence quote.

Dana Kim: I can take the interview. Available Wed/Thu next week. Prefer a quick 30min Zoom.

Leo Ortiz: I can help pull the benchmark data and visuals — will export the latency graphs and cost table. ETA EOD Monday.

Ben Carter: I'll draft the initial case study copy and a short social/tweet. Send the technical highlights I should call out.

Mira Patel: Key points: 4x latency drop, 30% cost after batching + KV cache, moved from multi-cloud warm pools to Redwood Dedicated for predictable tail latency. Avoid mentioning exact $ numbers. Also ask Priya for a quote on developer velocity.

Sam Huang: I'll coordinate with Priya. @Elena — after publish we should route inbound leads from the case page to EMEA SDR queue. Can you confirm owner?

Elena Vázquez: Yep — I'll own routing. Please add tag: source:case-aeromesh and region:emea. I want the lead form to ask for use-case and current monthly inference spend (optional).

Dana Kim: Draft interview questions: 1) What was the biggest pain before? 2) How did Redwood change your deployment lifecycle? 3) Anything surprising from cost/ops? 4) One-liner quote we can use in the header.

Mira Patel: Perfect. Ben, here's a starter social snippet — feel free to rewrite: 
```
AeroMesh accelerated inference and cut per-request latency by 4x with Redwood Dedicated — read how they balanced cost and reliability: https://redwood.ai/case/aeromesh
```

Ben Carter: Nice. I'll try two variants: one technical for dev channels, one exec-friendly for LinkedIn.

Sam Huang: Quick legal check — Priya's quote might reference a competitor product (she mentioned migration). Legal needs to review for attribution. @legal-bot can you advise?

legal-bot: Hi @Sam Huang — please submit the final quote text and any screenshots for a quick review. Note: avoid comparative claims like "faster than X" unless backed by a public benchmark. Turnaround typically 48 hours.

Mira Patel: Copy that. I'll ask Priya to keep the quote to product impact and developer experience, not naming competitors.

Priya Rao (AeroMesh) [via Sam]: "Redwood helped us reduce tail latency and pushed our deployment velocity forward — our engineers can iterate faster without firefighting infra." She said OK to a 1-2 sentence header quote.

Mira Patel: Thanks Sam. Ben — can you capture that as two variants: short header and slightly longer pull-quote?

Ben Carter: Drafts below: 
- Header: "Redwood reduced tail latency and accelerated our deployment velocity." 
- Pull: "Redwood helped us reduce tail latency and gave our engineers the confidence to iterate faster without spending cycles on infra."

Dana Kim: I made a minor edit to the pull-quote to remove passive phrasing — suggestion above looks good.

Sam Huang: Sent Priya the pull-quote for sign-off. Also scheduled interview for Thu 10:00 AM PST. Zoom link in calendar.

Mira Patel: Legal review request submitted with quote and two screenshots (latency graph, config snippet). Tracking doc: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aeromesh-case-draft

legal-bot: Received. Flagging for compliance: keeps PII out of screenshots, redact any customer IDs. Estimated approval: 2024-12-03 14:00 UTC.

Leo Ortiz: Uploaded graphs to Drive and added alt text. Added a short explainer table: 
```
Metric | Before | After
P50 latency | 90ms | 22ms
P95 latency | 420ms | 105ms
Cost per 1k reqs | baseline | -30% (batching + cache)
```

Mira Patel: Great — keep the "Cost per 1k reqs" line high-level as discussed.

Ben Carter: Draft landing copy is in the doc. Two CTAs: "Request a demo" (hosted leads) and "Talk to Sales" (enterprise). Can we add testimonial image and Priya headshot?

Elena Vázquez: For routing: hosted leads -> marketing@redwood.ai, enterprise -> enterprise-sales@redwood.ai, tag with source. I'll make sure EMEA SDR gets notified.

Dana Kim: Priya approved the quote with one tiny edit: replace "gave our engineers the confidence" -> "helped our engineers move faster" — she asked not to imply emotion. Sam, can you update?

Sam Huang: Updated the pull-quote and re-sent. Priya confirmed final approval.

legal-bot: Quick note: quote approved but please avoid publishing internal config snippets in screenshots. Redacted screenshot recommended. Approval code: LEG-2024-447.

ops-bot: Added campaign tracking tags to project: campaign=aero-a2, publish_window=2024-12-10_to_2024-12-17, assets=case_study, webinar.

Mira Patel: Ok timeline proposal: 
- Mon 2024-12-02: final copy and assets in Drive
- Wed 2024-12-04: design QA and legal clearance confirmed
- Tue 2024-12-10: publish case study & social drip (exec + dev posts)
- Wed 2024-12-11: webinar with Priya as guest (aim for 30-40 min)

Ben Carter: Webinar promo draft: subject lines and short blurb. Also we should make a tech deep-dive appendix for devs.

Mira Patel: One more thing — from inbound lead POV, please add a checkbox in the form: "Interested in Dedicated (predictable latency)" so we can prioritize. @Elena to implement.

Elena Vázquez: Will add checkbox and map to SDR playbook.

Sam Huang: Closing loop — interview recorded, transcript available in Drive, Priya ok with the webinar script Ben sent.

Mira Patel: Thank you everyone. I'll post the final publish checklist once legal confirms the redacted screenshots. :party_parrot: 

Ben Carter: Quick ask for copy: please confirm one-sentence hero line we can A/B test. Draft A: "AeroMesh cut tail latency 4x with Redwood Dedicated." Draft B: "Predictable latency at scale: how AeroMesh sped up inference and cut costs."

Dana Kim: I prefer B for the site, A for dev channels.

legal-bot: Final approval received for copy with redacted assets. Document LEG-2024-447. You are clear to publish per legal.

Mira Patel: Awesome — publish window confirmed for 2024-12-10. Elena will handle routing, ops-bot will tag campaign. Closing thread once the live URL is posted. 

ops-bot: Campaign tags applied and webhook added to /events/campaigns for lead routing.

Mira Patel: Live URL: https://redwood.ai/case/aeromesh — marking this thread closed. Thanks all! :tada:

text:
Mira Patel: Hey team — want to kick off a customer story for AeroMesh. They just finished a 6-week pilot and saw a 4x reduction in per-request latency and ~30% cost savings on our Dedicated tier. Can we lock down interviews and a publish plan?

Sam Huang: Awesome. Who's the main contact at AeroMesh?

Mira Patel: Priya Rao (CTO). She's okay with a short case study + 30-minute on-record interview. She offered a 2-3 sentence quote.

Dana Kim: I can take the interview. Available Wed/Thu next week. Prefer a quick 30min Zoom.

Leo Ortiz: I can help pull the benchmark data and visuals — will export the latency graphs and cost table. ETA EOD Monday.

Ben Carter: I'll draft the initial case study copy and a short social/tweet. Send the technical highlights I should call out.

Mira Patel: Key points: 4x latency drop, 30% cost after batching + KV cache, moved from multi-cloud warm pools to Redwood Dedicated for predictable tail latency. Avoid mentioning exact $ numbers. Also ask Priya for a quote on developer velocity.

Sam Huang: I'll coordinate with Priya. @Elena — after publish we should route inbound leads from the case page to EMEA SDR queue. Can you confirm owner?

Elena Vázquez: Yep — I'll own routing. Please add tag: source:case-aeromesh and region:emea. I want the lead form to ask for use-case and current monthly inference spend (optional).

Dana Kim: Draft interview questions: 1) What was the biggest pain before? 2) How did Redwood change your deployment lifecycle? 3) Anything surprising from cost/ops? 4) One-liner quote we can use in the header.

Mira Patel: Perfect. Ben, here's a starter social snippet — feel free to rewrite: 
```
AeroMesh accelerated inference and cut per-request latency by 4x with Redwood Dedicated — read how they balanced cost and reliability: https://redwood.ai/case/aeromesh
```

Ben Carter: Nice. I'll try two variants: one technical for dev channels, one exec-friendly for LinkedIn.

Sam Huang: Quick legal check — Priya's quote might reference a competitor product (she mentioned migration). Legal needs to review for attribution. @legal-bot can you advise?

legal-bot: Hi @Sam Huang — please submit the final quote text and any screenshots for a quick review. Note: avoid comparative claims like "faster than X" unless backed by a public benchmark. Turnaround typically 48 hours.

Mira Patel: Copy that. I'll ask Priya to keep the quote to product impact and developer experience, not naming competitors.

Priya Rao (AeroMesh) [via Sam]: "Redwood helped us reduce tail latency and pushed our deployment velocity forward — our engineers can iterate faster without firefighting infra." She said OK to a 1-2 sentence header quote.

Mira Patel: Thanks Sam. Ben — can you capture that as two variants: short header and slightly longer pull-quote?

Ben Carter: Drafts below: 
- Header: "Redwood reduced tail latency and accelerated our deployment velocity." 
- Pull: "Redwood helped us reduce tail latency and gave our engineers the confidence to iterate faster without spending cycles on infra."

Dana Kim: I made a minor edit to the pull-quote to remove passive phrasing — suggestion above looks good.

Sam Huang: Sent Priya the pull-quote for sign-off. Also scheduled interview for Thu 10:00 AM PST. Zoom link in calendar.

Mira Patel: Legal review request submitted with quote and two screenshots (latency graph, config snippet). Tracking doc: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aeromesh-case-draft

legal-bot: Received. Flagging for compliance: keeps PII out of screenshots, redact any customer IDs. Estimated approval: 2024-12-03 14:00 UTC.

Leo Ortiz: Uploaded graphs to Drive and added alt text. Added a short explainer table: 
```
Metric | Before | After
P50 latency | 90ms | 22ms
P95 latency | 420ms | 105ms
Cost per 1k reqs | baseline | -30% (batching + cache)
```

Mira Patel: Great — keep the "Cost per 1k reqs" line high-level as discussed.

Ben Carter: Draft landing copy is in the doc. Two CTAs: "Request a demo" (hosted leads) and "Talk to Sales" (enterprise). Can we add testimonial image and Priya headshot?

Elena Vázquez: For routing: hosted leads -> marketing@redwood.ai, enterprise -> enterprise-sales@redwood.ai, tag with source. I'll make sure EMEA SDR gets notified.

Dana Kim: Priya approved the quote with one tiny edit: replace "gave our engineers the confidence" -> "helped our engineers move faster" — she asked not to imply emotion. Sam, can you update?

Sam Huang: Updated the pull-quote and re-sent. Priya confirmed final approval.

legal-bot: Quick note: quote approved but please avoid publishing internal config snippets in screenshots. Redacted screenshot recommended. Approval code: LEG-2024-447.

ops-bot: Added campaign tracking tags to project: campaign=aero-a2, publish_window=2024-12-10_to_2024-12-17, assets=case_study, webinar.

Mira Patel: Ok timeline proposal: 
- Mon 2024-12-02: final copy and assets in Drive
- Wed 2024-12-04: design QA and legal clearance confirmed
- Tue 2024-12-10: publish case study & social drip (exec + dev posts)
- Wed 2024-12-11: webinar with Priya as guest (aim for 30-40 min)

Ben Carter: Webinar promo draft: subject lines and short blurb. Also we should make a tech deep-dive appendix for devs.

Mira Patel: One more thing — from inbound lead POV, please add a checkbox in the form: "Interested in Dedicated (predictable latency)" so we can prioritize. @Elena to implement.

Elena Vázquez: Will add checkbox and map to SDR playbook.

Sam Huang: Closing loop — interview recorded, transcript available in Drive, Priya ok with the webinar script Ben sent.

Mira Patel: Thank you everyone. I'll post the final publish checklist once legal confirms the redacted screenshots. :party_parrot: 

Ben Carter: Quick ask for copy: please confirm one-sentence hero line we can A/B test. Draft A: "AeroMesh cut tail latency 4x with Redwood Dedicated." Draft B: "Predictable latency at scale: how AeroMesh sped up inference and cut costs."

Dana Kim: I prefer B for the site, A for dev channels.

legal-bot: Final approval received for copy with redacted assets. Document LEG-2024-447. You are clear to publish per legal.

Mira Patel: Awesome — publish window confirmed for 2024-12-10. Elena will handle routing, ops-bot will tag campaign. Closing thread once the live URL is posted. 

ops-bot: Campaign tags applied and webhook added to /events/campaigns for lead routing.

Mira Patel: Live URL: https://redwood.ai/case/aeromesh — marking this thread closed. Thanks all! :tada:

source.thread:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — product wants a small but loud push for next Wed's webinar. Need X + LinkedIn schedule, 3 short thread angles, and the asset pack by EOD Friday. :rocket:
Diego Morales: I can draft the X threads. Can someone confirm the target audience + benchmark angle? is this more tech-performance or customer ROI?
Samir Patel: ROI + benchmark pls. Ops will love the lat/cost numbers. Also want one thread focused on dedicated capacity use-case.
Priya Nair: I'll own LinkedIn copy + asset pack. Can @Diego share thread drafts by tomorrow noon? I need time to adapt for LinkedIn. :calendar:
Ava Chen: Target audience = platform/infra engineers + ML infra leads. Tone = punchy, data-first, 3 CTAs: webinar sign-up, benchmark read, dedicated trial.
Diego Morales: Got it. I'll spin three angles: 1) "latency wins" benchmark highlight, 2) "cost/throughput tradeoffs" case study, 3) "migration story" (hosted->dedicated). Will include one carousel concept for LinkedIn.
Samir Patel: Also flag the inbound routing: the webinar form should tag 'webinar_2026_03' and send to HubSpot list 'webinar-regs'. We need SDR follow-up within 24h. @Priya can you confirm form fields?
Priya Nair: I'll update the form. Fields: name, email, company, role, use_case, consent. Add hidden utm and "interested_in_dedicated" checkbox. Tagging plan noted.
Ava Chen: For engagement follow-ups: we'll have an engagement rota during the live thread — @Diego + @Samir take X, @Priya handle LinkedIn comments, Jules (marketing-bot) will post reminder 30m before. :eyes:
Jules (marketing-bot): Reminder scheduled: "Webinar: Serving at Scale — starts in 30m" at 2026-03-25T14:30:00Z. Will post to #marketing and crosspost to #events.
Diego Morales: Draft threads (short):
```
Thread A (Latency wins):
1/ We measured p95 reduce from 420ms -> 180ms by switching kernels + batching. Here’s how we did it. (link)
2/ Cost impact: 28% lower per-token with prefix caching.
3/ Join our webinar to see config examples + live Q&A. #inference #llmops
```

```
Thread B (Cost & throughput):
1/ How to serve 2k reqs/s without blowing budget.
2/ Autoscaling + quantization tradeoffs we used.
3/ RSVP for benchmarks + config files. (link) :chart_with_upwards_trend:
```

```
Thread C (Migration story):
1/ Moved a customer from hosted API -> dedicated pool with zero downtime.
2/ Lessons on routing and fallbacks.
3/ Watch the webinar for rollout checklist. #devrel
```
```
Priya Nair: These are great. I'll turn Thread A into a 3-card LinkedIn carousel and reuse B as a short article. Can you drop the images/assets here by EOD? I need high-res PNGs + short captions.
Samir Patel: I'll export benchmark charts and the kernel comparison table. Attaching quick CSV snippet for the sched team to ingest:
```
post_time,channel,copy,asset
2026-03-25T14:00:00Z,X,"Thread A lead",latency_chart.png
2026-03-25T14:05:00Z,LinkedIn,"Carousel: latency wins",carousel_imgs.zip
```

Ava Chen: Nice. One more: please include 2-3 suggested replies for the live thread so our SDRs can copy-paste. e.g., "Thanks for joining! If you're evaluating dedicated capacity, drop your use-case — we'll connect you with an engineer."
Diego Morales: I'll add canned replies and a short FAQ doc.
Priya Nair: FYI, scheduling sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-example (edit access for marketing + ops). Update your rows.
Samir Patel: Who's on live duty for first 60 mins after post? I can cover 14:00-14:30 UTC, Diego can do 14:30-15:00.
Ava Chen: Perfect. I'll notify SDRs to watch for tagged leads and connect to HubSpot queue. Let's aim to have everything in origin thread by EOD Friday. :thumbsup:
Diego Morales: I'll post draft threads to this thread by 12:00 UTC tomorrow.
Priya Nair: I'll assemble the asset pack and share a zip + captions by EOD Friday.
Jules (marketing-bot): Post scheduled: 2026-03-25T14:00:00Z to X (queued).
Ava Chen: Thanks team — close loop once posted.
Diego Morales: Thanks!
Priya Nair: On it. :white_check_mark:

source.text:
Maria Chen: Need a tight benchmark narrative for the launch page + webinar signups. Goal: make the latency vs cost tradeoff understandable in one glance for both engineers and procurement. Ask: 3 headline hooks, hero visual (1), appendix visuals (2), and inbound routing for demo vs download. Who can take which?

Liam Ortiz: I'll draft hooks. My angle: 1) "Measureable latency, measurable savings" 2) "Engineering-grade performance, procurement-grade economics" 3) "Don't choose between speed and cost" — short and testable.

Nora Singh: Visual recs: Hero = bivariate scatter (p95 latency on X, $/1M tokens on Y) with shaded "operational sweet spot"; Appendix 1 = waterfall bars showing cost components (compute, batching, kv-cache); Appendix 2 = small multiples of same model with quantization/batching toggles. PNGs in drive by EOD.

Ethan Park: Routing: use hidden field "lead_intent" = {demo, benchmark}. If demo or company_size>100 -> route to SDR; else -> auto-email with benchmark PDF + webinar invite. Add 1pt lead_score for webinar RSVP and +2 if they request demo after download. Quick pseudo: 

```py
intent = form['lead_intent']
size = int(form.get('company_size',0))
if intent=='demo' or size>100:
  assign='sdr_queue'
else:
  assign='growth_nurture'
```

Maria Chen: Nice. Two social hooks needed (one technical, one outcome). Also one-paragraph webinar blurb. Deadline: assets by Tue 17:00 so growth can draft emails.

Liam Ortiz: Socials: Tech -> "See where latency and unit-cost meet. Full benchmark inside." Outcome -> "Same throughput, lower cost — production inference that pays for itself." Will post drafts by Monday.

Nora Singh: Will attach simple legend-friendly mock (colorblind-safe) and an annotated version. Suggest hero alt text: "Scatter of p95 latency vs cost per million tokens with highlighted sweet spot".

ops-bot: Reminder: Marketing sync 2024-07-02 10:00 UTC added to calendar.

Maria Chen: Decision: use the scatter as hero, waterfall and small multiples in report. CTAs: Primary = Request demo, Secondary = Download full benchmark + Webinar RSVP. Routing as Ethan outlined. I'll compile playbook and tag tasks. Thanks.
