source.text:
Ethan Park: Starting greenroom + triage playbook thread for Sept webinar 'Practical Routing & Recovery'. Need to finalize greenroom roles, captioning, and attendee routing.
Maya Ortiz: Agree. We should list primary roles: host, guest speaker, stage mgr, producer, captions vendor contact, captions monitor, and tech lead.
Ravi Desai: I'll be tech lead. Quick ask: do we want live captions + translated captions (EN->ES) for APAC folks? Adds ~10-12s latency.
Zoey Kim: Yes to captions. I'll confirm vendor availability and cost. Also propose a secondary captions monitor (Sam) in greenroom to watch for misalignments.
Sam Garcia: Happy to monitor captions and flag any hallucinated text. Also can patch near-live translated captions if vendor lags.
Ethan Park: Greenroom timeline idea: 30 min pre-show: audio check, slide load, fallback clip sanity, captions test. 15 min pre-show: speaker bios + mic check on stage dial. 5 min pre-start: final CTAs and poll live test.
Maya Ortiz: Add a 2-min intro script and a 90s compliance blurb for privacy (what we record, how we use Q&A). I'll draft and post.
Ravi Desai: Tech checklist in short:
```
- Confirm RTMP/Zoom ingest + recording path
- Verify slideshare permissions for host and producer
- Run quick infer runtime smoke test (p95 < 350ms)
- Confirm fallback clips accessible by stage mgr
- Test inbound form webhook and SDR routing (dry run)
```
Zoey Kim: Captions-bot: please run a 60s live caption test against vendor and post transcript link. @captions-bot any ETA?
captions-bot: Vendor test scheduled for 2pm PT today. Will post transcript and latency numbers after run.
Ethan Park: Fallback policy (short): if live demo p95 > threshold OR API error rate > 1% for 2+ mins -> swap to recorded demo clip + host does a walkthrough of the recorded output; producer posts pre-seeded lab responses in chat.
Sam Garcia: Pre-seeded lab: I'll prepare 10 canonical responses per demo scenario and upload as JSONL to shared folder. Will include timestamps and short notes for host to read.
Maya Ortiz: Audience routing plan: registration form fields = company, role, monthly tokens, top pain (cost/latency/ops/quality), deploy interest. Routing rules: role=engineer -> SDR-tech; role=platform lead -> AE; >5M tokens -> AE-priority. We'll capture demo preference for follow-up.
Ravi Desai: For privacy we should hide tokens field to external viewers in the recording. Producer: blur form screenshots in the recording.
Zoey Kim: Also capture timezone in form -> automated email with local follow-up times. I'll add a timezone dropdown.
Ethan Park: Rehearsal plan: Full run Sep 5 10:00am PT, Tech-check Sep 8 9:30am PT. @calendar-bot please create events with greenroom dial-in, captions test window, and fallback clip upload link.
calendar-bot: Events created: "Sep 5 Full Rehearsal" and "Sep 8 Tech Check". Greenroom dial-in and upload links attached.
Maya Ortiz: Post-webinar nurture plan quick notes: 1) send recording + captions within 24h, 2) segmented CTA: cost-estimate (if pain=cost), ops-consult (if pain=ops), product-trial (if role=pm), 3) schedule AE follow-up for >5M tokens. I'll draft email sequences.
Sam Garcia: I'll validate SDR routing rules and run dry-run tests into a staging queue. Will post results here by EOD.
Ethan Park: Great. Last ask: speakers upload 2-paragraph bio, headshot, and a 90s fallback clip by Aug 28. Locking playbook unless objections. :white_check_mark:

source.messages:
Alice Nguyen: Quick pulse — we need a pitch rotation for the launch webinar next Tue. I sketched an outreach order: top-tier press -> analyst firms -> trade outlets -> follow-ups. Thoughts?
Ben Carter: sounds good. can we add a separate rapid-follow slot for APAC times? webinar is 10am PT so late night for EMEA/APAC.
Priya Shah: yes please. propose: live exec briefing for US press 2 days before, then analyst roundtable the day after. analysts prefer smaller groups.
Liam O'Connor: I'll map availability. do we have list of analysts who asked for hands-on with Dedicated/Private? need to prioritize.
Alice Nguyen: good call. @Liam can you flag folks who requested on-prem/private trials? we should pair them with infra-focused spokespeople.
Liam O'Connor: will do. Sanjay (GCP infra lead) is free Thurs afternoon — should we slot him into analyst roundtable?
Priya Shah: yes. also we need a cleared quote for the press kit. Draft below — please approve or suggest edits.
Priya Shah: "Redwood Inference delivers predictable inference latency at scale and can cut per-token serving costs by up to 40% on typical customer workloads."
Ben Carter: strong. request legal to confirm the "40%" number. who owns the benchmark source?
Alice Nguyen: research/benchmarks owns it — @Sara can we get sign-off from Research that 40% is defensible and the benchmark link to include?
Sara Patel: I can ping Research now. quick preference: include short methodology note or an appendix link, otherwise press will push for details.
pressops-bot: Note: embargo window set to 2026-04-04T17:00:00Z per Launch Plan. Do not publish until embargo lift.
Priya Shah: thanks bot. also we need a quote from CTO for the analyst brief. draft: "Our focus is making inference predictable and economical across clouds and on-prem — customers get production SLAs without rearchitecting." thoughts?
Ben Carter: make it tighter — drop "making" and say "We deliver predictable, economical inference across cloud and on-prem, so teams ship faster without rearchitecting."
Priya Shah: updated. sending to CTO for 2-hour turnaround.
Liam O'Connor: schedule matrix: I'll create a two-day slot window for analyst calls (Wed/Thu). want 30m blocks; give each firm one block and allow follow-up 15m the next day.
Alice Nguyen: perfect. @Ben can you draft two email templates: 1) top-tier press invite + exec briefing, 2) analyst invite that mentions optional hands-on. keep word count tight.
Ben Carter: on it. will link calendar + attach whitepaper. any embargo language specifics?
Priya Shah: include embargo timestamp and a one-line on backgrounders available after briefing. and a fact-check contact: press@redwood-inference.com.

Alice Nguyen: great — incorporate that link into the press kit. final ask: can we get legal to approve both quoted lines and the 40% claim by EOD? If yes, we can lock invites tomorrow morning.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Ok team — picking final invite segments and locking the rehearsal schedule for the ‘Cost vs Latency: Practical LLM Serving’ webinar. Quick plan: two rehearsals (content + tech), one dress rehearsal with slides. Who can make the earliest slot tomorrow?

Jon Park: I can do the tech check tomorrow 10:30 PT. Will verify staging stream + captions.

Priya Singh: Content run (script + transitions) works for me at 11:30 PT. Can we keep rehearsals to 45m each? Need time for slide edits after.

Marta Lopez: I’ll join the dress rehearsal at 3pm PT — RSVP list shows most panelists available then. Also need final speaker bios by EOD so we can update the landing page.

Ava Chen: Good. Action items: Jon -> tech checklist + streaming link; Priya -> final script + 5 bullet Qs for panel; Marta -> collect bios and headshots. I'll split invite segments and share copy.

Jon Park: Draft tech checklist here:
```
- Verify RTMP to staging (rtmp://staging.redwood/video)
- Confirm mic levels for each panelist (ask for test clip)
- Enable auto-captions, language = en-US
- Test screen-share for deck presenter
- Record locally & cloud, confirm retention settings
- Confirm fallback room + dial-in (Zoom) if stream fails
```
I’ll paste the staging link in #eng-infra once ready.

Priya Singh: For invites, propose 3 segments: (1) Product engineers at startups (solution-focused), (2) Platform/infra leads (SLA/cost), (3) ML/Research teams (quantization, evals). Each gets slightly different subject line + CTA.

Marta Lopez: Love that. Draft subject lines:
- Engineers: "Reduce per-token cost without doubling latency — real tactics"
- Platform: "How to guarantee latency at scale: routing & caching in practice"
- ML: "Serving best practices: quantization, KV caching, and eval guards"
Can we A/B subject line for segment 1?

Ava Chen: Yes A/B for segment 1 — keep everything else static. Marta please add UTM params by segment. Also, inbound lead routing: send high-intent signups (enterprise email domains or >5 seats) to sales queue immediately, the rest to nurture. Priya — can you add a quick checkbox on the registration form for "interested in Dedicated or Private"?

Priya Singh: Yep — adding a hidden field for seat count + checkbox for Dedicated/Private interest. Also adding a short multiple-choice on primary priority (latency, cost, reliability). That will help routing.

Jon Park: Streaming notes: we should enable lower-bitrate fallback for APAC viewers. I’ll confirm CDN edge config.

Ava Chen: Rehearsal run-of-show (draft):
```
- 00:00-02:00 - Host intro (Ava) + housekeeping
- 02:00-12:00 - Product demo (Jon + demo VM)
- 12:00-24:00 - Panel discussion (Priya moderates): 3x 4-min topics
- 24:00-28:00 - Live Q&A (moderated)
- 28:00-30:00 - CTA + close (Marta)
```
Keep demo <=10m and one backup demo video in case of network issues.

Marta Lopez: Adding to that — slide deck must include a 30s slide with the follow-up offer + link and a reminder to opt-in to be contacted. Also mark the slide that will be used as thumbnail for the recording.

Ava Chen: Decision: recording will be gated — require email to view. Sales wants 48h lead export after event. Sam (deploy-bot) can you confirm automated attendee export is scheduled? @Sam (deploy-bot)

Sam (deploy-bot): Automated export scheduled: attendee CSV to /marketing/exports/ with 3-tier tags (segment, interest, seat_count). Delivery scheduled T+0h for enterprise-tier, T+48h for others.

Priya Singh: Rehearsal checklist for panelists to send: 1) test mic clip, 2) high-res headshot, 3) 2-3 talking points, 4) slide if presenting. Send a template email?

Marta Lopez: I have a template — will personalize and send now. Will tag panelists who haven't replied.

Ava Chen: Can we do a 15m pre-show tech check on the day-of for presenters? Important to confirm presenter deck remote control works.

Jon Park: Absolutely. I’ll schedule pre-show at T-15 and leave a runbook in the staging room.

Priya Singh: One more: we should record a 90s highlight clip during rehearsal to use for post-event ads. Quick permission ask to speakers.

Marta Lopez: Got permission from two panelists already. Will confirm the rest in the template.

Ava Chen: OK — timeline:
- Today EOD: final invite copy segmented + subject lines (Marta)
- Tomorrow 10:30 PT: tech check (Jon)
- Tomorrow 11:30 PT: content run (Priya)
- Day-of T-15m: pre-show tech check (Jon)
- Day-of 3:00 PT: dress rehearsal (all)
Everyone good? :thumbsup:

Jon Park: :thumbsup: I’ll post the staging RTMP and CDN notes in channel when ready.

Priya Singh: :thumbsup: I’ll upload the script and slide cue notes to the shared drive.

Marta Lopez: :thumbsup: Sending bios + invite segments now.

Ava Chen: Great. Thanks team — I’ll follow up with calendar invites and the registration link.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick update  I uploaded a one-pager draft for the enterprise launch focused on secure deployment choices, SLA callouts, and a short benchmark soundbite. Link: https://docs.google.com/onepager-draft

Marco Diaz: Thanks  skimmed. Recommend we call out p95 latencies on the one-pager; keep p99 in appendix for large-enterprise asks.

Ava Chen: Agreed. Draft line: "Typical p95 token latency: Hosted ~230ms, Dedicated ~180ms, Private (on-prem tuned) ~210ms."

Priya Nair: Add a security checklist at top: encryption (rest/in-transit), KMS/HSM support, SOC2, audit logs, VPC peering. Sales wants that first.

Sam Patel: Also add a short availability note: "Automatic regional fallback + model-variant routing helps preserve SLA during capacity events."

Jasmine Li: I wrote two headline options for webinar landing (reliability vs economics). For enterprise webinar we should lead with reliability. Will put both in the doc.

Ava Chen: Also proposing simple inbound-routing rules for leads (example in doc). Engineers will validate mapping but marketing needs the initial rules and tracking tag campaign=secure-inference-q2.

Marco Diaz: I'll prep an anonymized benchmark pack if Legal asks for sources.

Priya Nair: For the sales sheet include a 3-question decision prompt: "Data isolation? -> Private. Committed throughput? -> Dedicated. Quick start? -> Hosted."

Sam Patel: I'll draft a one-slide objections cheat sheet (cost, compliance, uptime) and drop by EOD.

Ava Chen: Please do. LaunchBot, schedule a dry-run.

LaunchBot: Calendar event created: "Enterprise launch dry-run" on 2026-04-08 10:00 PT. Attendees: Ava Chen, Marco Diaz, Priya Nair, Sam Patel, Jasmine Li. Reminder 24h before. :calendar:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick check — pulled the weekly dashboard slice for webinar funnel. Landing page sessions up 22% after the paid push but conversion to registrants dropped ~18%. Anyone else seeing a jump in bounce on /webinar/signup?
Jamal Ortiz: I saw that too. looks like paid traffic is higher but CTR on the CTA is lower. Could be mismatch in variants. :thinking_face:
Priya Nair: Did we change the UTM mapping for the paid creative? I remember QAing a tag last night.
Elena Kuznetsova: Not from content side — creative is same. I can re-export the ad UTM list.
analytics-bot: Automated snapshot: GA4 sessions: 12,432 (+22%), Registrations: 1,045 (-18%), SFDC leads created: 980 (-20%).
Ava Chen: SFDC vs GA4 discrepancy still ~6% gap, which is expected but the drop pattern looks correlated to campaign start time (2026-03-18 10:00 UTC).
Jamal Ortiz: Can someone run the quick query on source/medium vs landing_page? I want to see if a routed redirect is stripping utm_campaign. Small SQL: ```SELECT source_medium, landing_page, COUNT(*) AS sessions FROM events WHERE event_date between '2026-03-11' and '2026-03-18' GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY sessions DESC LIMIT 20;```
Priya Nair: running now — will paste results in a sec.
analytics-bot: Query started by Priya Nair (Clickhouse) — ETA ~12s.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick thread to finalise post-event operations for the June roundtable: registration page, Zoom setup, recording artifacts, and replay routing. Please read and ack.

Priya Singh: Can Zoom produce separate audio tracks + auto-transcript? Need clean transcript for captions.

Ava Chen: Yes — Zoom cloud recording ON, separate audio track enabled, auto-transcript ON. Naming: redwood_vr_2026-06-17_{session}.

Diego Martinez: I'll be host. Need co-host to manage breakouts & start recording if I'm delayed. Samir?

Samir Patel: I can co-host and take notes in the shared doc.

Ava Chen: HubSpot form required fields: email, company, job_title, use_case (dropdown), utm_source. Add GDPR checkbox consent_to_contact.

Priya Singh: Add hidden marketing_channel field for UTM mapping.

Maya (growth): Replay plan: attendees -> unlisted replay link immediately. Non-attendees: gated replay if lead_score >= 20; else standard nurture drip.

Diego Martinez: Implementation via HubSpot smart lists + workflow checking `attended` and `lead_score`.

Ava Chen: Workflow sketch: Trigger=form_submit -> if attended=true send attendee_replay_email; else if lead_score>=20 send gated_replay_request_email; else enroll in 3-email nurture.

Priya Singh: SDR routing: non-attendee AND lead_score>=40 -> create SDR task assigned to queue EMEA-SDR, SLA 48h, tag `roundtable_followup`.

Samir Patel: I'll implement the task automation and SLA timer.

Ava Chen: Post-event assets needed: 3 short clips (30-90s), 1 newsletter highlight, full transcript + burned-in captions. Zoe, can pipeline produce captions + clips?

Zoe (Ops Bot): Yes. Processing will upload to gs://redwood-marketing/roundtables/2026-06-17/ and post links once ready.

Maya (growth): Need at least one clip + replay URL by EOD Monday for scheduling. I'll draft email variants and CTA tests.

Diego Martinez: Zoom posts recordings ~1hr after session. Extraction job: ./scripts/extract_clips.sh <recording_id>. I'll run it when rec ID appears.

Ava Chen: All public links must include utm_campaign=vr_roundtable_2026&utm_medium=email&utm_source=post_event.

Priya Singh: I'll update form + workflows today and push attended event to Redshift for analytics joins.

Samir Patel: For gated replay, prefer a tokenized 1-click access link. Confirm token TTL?

Ava Chen: Token TTL suggested = 14 days. Priya to confirm with Security.

Priya Singh: Will check with Security and report by EOD.

Ava Chen: Slack announcement plan: post in #marketing and #product with short blurb and console event: event=roundtable.replay.published, properties={session, recording_id, clips}.

Zoe (Ops Bot): After processing I'll post: transcript.txt, captions.srt, clips/{1,2,3}.mp4, highlight.mp4.

Maya (growth): I'll prepare 3 CTA variants (Register / Watch / Request demo) and share in the doc.

Diego Martinez: Will confirm host/co-host + final preflight 1hr before start. :thumbsup:

EventBot: Recording started 2026-06-17T16:00:00Z recording_id=rec_98432

Ava Chen: Final checklist for this thread: 
- [ ] Zoom host/co-host (Diego/Samir)
- [ ] HubSpot form + GDPR checkbox (Priya)
- [ ] Workflows + post-event emails (Maya/Priya)
- [ ] Captions/transcript/clips (Zoe)
- [ ] SDR routing automation (Samir)
- [ ] Slack announcement + analytics event (Ava)

Ava Chen: Ping me for any blockers. Thanks everyone!

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick thread to lock the post-event flow for Thursday's virtual roundtable — registration, Zoom, recording, and replay routing. Short checklist + asks below.

Priya Singh: Quick q — can we get auto-transcript & separate audio tracks from Zoom? Need transcript for captioning.

Ava Chen: Yes. Zoom cloud recording = ON, recording layout = gallery + shared screen, audio: separate track. Auto-transcript = ON. Recording naming convention: redwood_vr_2026-06-17_{session}.

Diego Martinez: I can host. Need a co-host for breakout rooms & to start recording if I'm delayed — Samir?

Samir Patel: Happy to co-host and run breakout timers. I'll take notes in the shared doc.

Ava Chen: HubSpot form fields we must capture: email, company, job_title, use_case (dropdown), utm_source. Include `consent_to_contact` checkbox for GDPR.

Priya Singh: Also add a hidden field `marketing_channel` for UTM capture so we can split routing.

Maya (growth): Replay strategy: attendees get an unlisted replay link in post-event email. Non-attendees get gated access depending on lead score — propose gated full recording at score >= 20, immediate nurture below that. Thoughts?

Diego Martinez: Works for me. Implementation: HubSpot smart list + workflow to check `attended` property and `lead_score`.

Ava Chen: HubSpot workflow pseudo: 
```
Trigger: form_submission
If attended = true -> send: replay_attendee_email (include calendar + unlisted link)
Else if lead_score >= 20 -> send: gated_replay_request_email
Else -> enroll in 3-email nurture drip
```

Priya Singh: SDR routing: if non-attendee AND lead_score >= 40 -> create task assigned to SDR queue 'EMEA-SDR' with SLA 48h. Add tag `roundtable_followup`.

Samir Patel: I'll build the task automation. Confirming SLA = 48 hours.

Ava Chen: Production asks for post-event assets: 3 short clips (30–90s) for socials, 1 newsletter highlight, full transcript + burned-in captions. Zoe — can we get caption burn-in and clips from transcript pipeline?

Zoe (Ops Bot): Caption/transcript pipeline is ready. Files will be uploaded to gs://redwood-marketing/roundtables/2026-06-17/. I'll emit links when processing finishes.

Maya (growth): Need at least 1 clip + the replay URL by EOD Monday for social scheduling. I'll draft email copy now and drop in the doc.

Diego Martinez: Zoom cloud usually posts recording within ~1 hour of session end. I'll trigger the extraction job once recording_id available: `./scripts/extract_clips.sh <recording_id>`.

Ava Chen: Naming + UTM for shared links: use utm_campaign=vr_roundtable_2026&utm_medium=email&utm_source=post_event. Please include query params on all public URLs.

Priya Singh: I'll update the form and workflows today and post the change log. Also will add the `attended` event push to Redshift so analytics can join sessions <> leads.

Samir Patel: For the gated replay flow, confirm we need a 1-click access email (tokenized link) vs full re-registration. Tokenized link preferred for conversion.

Ava Chen: Tokenized link — yes. But require double-check with security for token expiry = 14 days. Priya, can you confirm token TTL?

Priya Singh: Will confirm with Security by EOD.

Ava Chen: Slack posting plan: once clips + replay URL are ready, post in #marketing and #product with short blurb + analytics events to console: event = roundtable.replay.published, properties = {session, recording_id, clips}.

Zoe (Ops Bot): After processing, I'll post these links: transcript, captions.srt, clips (3), highlight.mp4.

Maya (growth): I'll prepare 3 short CTA variants for testing (Register -> Watch -> Request Demo). Will share in doc by tomorrow.

Diego Martinez: I'll drop final confirmation in this thread 1 hour before the session start. :thumbsup: 

EventBot: Recording started 2026-06-17 16:00 UTC — recording_id=rec_98432

Ava Chen: Got it — thanks all. Final checklist for thread (checkboxes below are for tracking):
- [ ] Zoom host/co-host set (Diego/Samir)
- [ ] Form updated + GDPR checkbox (Priya)
- [ ] Post-event emails + gating workflows (Maya/Priya)
- [ ] Captions/transcripts + clips (Zoe)
- [ ] SDR routing & task automation (Samir)
- [ ] Slack announcement + console event (Ava)

Ava Chen: If anyone hits blockers ping me directly and I'll escalate. Thanks!

source.text:
Samir Patel: Kicking off planning for the Bridge Studio micro-summit on May 12. Goal: onsite demo + embargoed benchmark release aligned with a follow-up webinar. Need speaker shortlist, swag qty, lead routing, and messaging. Who owns the benchmark narrative/PR? :rocket:

Priya Rao: I can draft the benchmark narrative. Should we position it as 'Throughput-first, cost-savvy' with 2 charts? Need the eval set by EoW.

Nora Kim: I'll own the webinar flow + landing page. Webinar target: Tue May 19 11am PT. CTA = 'Request a dedicated capacity trial' -> tag leads with campaign=bridge-studio.

Diego Flores: Swag proposal: 400 shirts, 500 stickers, 200 mini powerbanks. Budget ok? Need sizes by 5/1. Also request table banners and a mic stand.

Samir Patel: @Priya please include a short blurb we can use in booth handouts and social. Keep hero <70 chars. Draft: 'Redwood: Inference that scales — lower cost, consistent latency.' Feedback?

Priya Rao: Draft hero lines:
- 'Predictable inference. Lower OPEX.'
- 'Ship LLM features with production-grade latency.'
I prefer the 2nd one. Use the first as subline.

Nora Kim: Lead routing plan: onsite QR -> Typeform -> Zapier -> HubSpot. Map fields: campaign=bridge-studio, source=booth, interest={dedicated, private, hosted}. Assign SDR pool 'EMEA-APAC' for APAC leads based on phone country code.

Samir Patel: Action items:
- Priya: benchmark narrative + 2 slide charts (due 2026-04-26)
- Nora: webinar landing + flow + email copy (due 2026-04-28)
- Diego: swag order + vendor quotes (due 2026-04-24)
- Ava Chen: social launch copy + cadence (due 2026-04-27)

Ava Chen: I'll run a 3-post cadence: pre-summit teaser, embargoed benchmark post (aligned with webinar), and post-webinar highlights + CTA. Teaser copy idea: 'Meet us at Bridge Studio — benchmarking live demos & giveaways'.

Priya Rao: For the benchmark narrative, quick bullets to highlight:
- testbed: 8k context, mixed prompt shapes
- metric: median, p95 token latency and tokens/sec
- comparison: stock open model X vs quantized Y on same infra
I'll put charts on Google Slides and share.

Samir Patel: Onsite staffing: need 3 people during peak (10:30-14:30). @Diego confirm volunteers. Also need AV for live demo stream.

Diego Flores: Confirmed 4 volunteers (Samir, Diego, Ava, external AE). AV request submitted to venue.

Nora Kim: Webinar reminders cadence: T-3 days, T-1 day, T-1 hour. Use personalized subject lines per region. Email snippet:
'Join our live demo + benchmark walkthrough. We'll show latency & cost tradeoffs and leave time for Q&A. RSVP to secure your spot.'

Samir Patel: For social/press embargo: embargo lift = webinar start time. We'll publish benchmark blog + thread at that time. @Priya add header 'Embargo until May 19 11:00 PT' on the doc.

Priya Rao: Noted and will add.

DeployBot: Reminder scheduled: 'send social embargo post' at 2026-05-19T11:00:00-07:00

Ava Chen: For booth handout copy, use this 70-char hero: 'Ship LLM features with production-grade latency.' Subline: 'Predictable inference across hosted, dedicated, and private.'

Nora Kim: Post-webinar lead triage: auto-tag leads with 'webinar_attended' if they join and set priority if engagement>60%. SDR follow-up windows: 48h for hot, 5 days for warm.

Samir Patel: Anything else? Need shipping address for swag and the lead-scanner iPad.

Diego Flores: I'll DM logistics details and confirm shipping address.

Ava Chen: Sending final social copy by EoD Monday.

Priya Rao: Uploading draft slides to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake-link

Samir Patel: Great — thanks everyone :raised_hands:

source.thread:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — final checklist for tomorrow's webinar (10:00am PT). Need owners for: registration confirmation copy, Zoom recording settings, captioning + timestamped clip handoff to content ops.
Raj Patel: I can take Zoom recording + upload to S3 and tag with event slug. Will push within 30m of session end. :thumbsup:
Lina Gomez: I'll own the registration confirmation copy and post-event email. Draft ready, can send for a quick review in 30.
Ava Chen: Perfect. @Raj — use the 'marketing-webinars/2026-q1' S3 prefix. Please include speaker timestamps in the file metadata.
Raj Patel: Got it. My upload script:
```
# quick snippet
aws s3 cp recording.mp4 s3://redwood-marketing/marketing-webinars/2026-q1/${EVENT_SLUG}/recording.mp4 --acl private
# then tag
aws s3api put-object-tagging --bucket redwood-marketing --key marketing-webinars/2026-q1/${EVENT_SLUG}/recording.mp4 --tagging 'TagSet=[{Key=event,Value=${EVENT_SLUG}},{Key=speaker,Value=${SPEAKER_LAST}}]'
```
Lina Gomez: Nice. For the confirmation email please include: link to calendar add, short agenda, access info (Zoom link + passcode), and caption availability note. I'll include CTA to watch recording.
Sam Ortiz: Ops checklist for Zoom — recording: cloud enabled, record active speaker + gallery, auto-save chat, enable 'Allow host to record' for co-host, and enable live auto-caption. Also set interpretation OFF (we don't have interpreters). I'll set a 2nd co-host (Raj) so he can start/stop and grab cloud file.
Maya Li: Content ops: after Raj uploads, I will generate a 3-min highlight clip + 30s social cut, and pull transcript for quote snippets. ETA: 2 hours after file upload.
Ava Chen: Captioning — we prefer vendor accurate captions. Raj, leave cloud VTT in place; ops-bot will auto-export raw captions. We'll send VTT to caption vendor for cleanup.
ops-bot: Reminder scheduled: 2026-05-14T09:00:00Z — Run preflight checklist (mic levels, co-host permissions, cloud recording) — pinging #eng-oncall and #marketing 15m before.
Raj Patel: One question — registration provider is Typeform. Do we need to map custom fields to HubSpot?
Lina Gomez: Yes. Map: email -> contact.email, company -> company, role -> job_title, consent_checkbox -> marketing_consent. Also add "event_source=webinar_2026q1" UTM hidden field.
Maya Li: For clips, use these markers: intro (0:00-1:00), demo-start (12:20-15:00), faq (44:30-52:00). I'll confirm exact timestamps after transcript.
Ava Chen: Approvals: Lina will send confirmation copy now, Maya will take the finalized transcript quotes to craft social posts. Raj, after upload please drop the S3 link here and tag @Maya and @Lina.
Raj Patel: Will do. Also enabling Zoom setting: 'Optimize for video clip' in cloud recording so we get better encoding for clips.
Lina Gomez: Quick check — do we have permission language for on-demand distribution? Need to confirm with legal.
Ava Chen: Legal approved standard webinar terms last week. I'll paste the approved snippet in a follow-up.
Raj Patel: Upload script completed. I'll attach link in thread when done. :eyes:
Maya Li: Small ask — can we get the transcript JSON export as well (timestamps per sentence)? Makes clip selection faster.
Raj Patel: I'll include raw VTT + JSON (transcript-generator output).
Ava Chen: Sweet. Last thing: post-event nurture flow — 24h email with recording, 72h reminder for those who didn't watch, and HubSpot list segmentation for attendees vs registrants-only. Lina will wire the email cadence.
Lina Gomez: On it. Draft go-ahead in 20.
Ava Chen: Thanks team — feel free to ping me if anything slips. :pray:

Ava Chen: Quick check — launch blog + press window timing. We need the blog live, press e-mail out, and social push sequenced. Can ops confirm publish ETA for the blog build?
Priya Patel: Blog build is green on staging. Final QA done at 14:00 PT. Planning publish at 15:00 PT unless legal flags.
Marco Rivera: PR list ready. Targeting press send at T+10 min after blog (15:10 PT). Want to give reporters the live link and assets.
Jordan Kim: Social queue is drafted. Will hold first organic post at T+20 (15:20 PT) and paid at T+30 (15:30 PT). Need final hero image from design by 14:15.
Li Wei: Design uploaded hero_v3.png to /shared/launch-assets. Small tweak done. Confirmed alt text and dimensions.
Priya Patel: Legal quick thumbs up on the quote; asked for one minor edit in CEO line, pushed to doc. Fix in place at 14:25.
Ava Chen: Great — can commops-bot confirm that the live URL and canonical tags will be accurate on publish? Don't want redirects to break reporters. :eyes:
commops-bot: Deployment scheduled: blog:redwood-launch (v2.3.1) -> prod at 15:00:00 PT. Live URL = https://redwood.inference/blog/launch-2026. Canonical meta present. Will post publish webhook to #marketing.
Marco Rivera: Copy for press email is final. Want to add one data point from benchmarks — the 99th p95 latency stat. Priya, legal said okay?
Priya Patel: Legal cleared the specific benchmark phrase but flagged we avoid implying SLA. Use "observed" and link to benchmark appendix.
Jordan Kim: Social captions updated to use "observed" phrasing. Also adding an explainer thread planned at 15:25 PT.
Li Wei: Confirming image alt and open graph test on staging. OG image resolves and looks good in card preview.
Ava Chen: Thanks team. Final checklist for deploy: (1) blog publish at 15:00 PT, (2) press send at 15:10 PT, (3) first social at 15:20 PT, (4) explainer thread at 15:25 PT. Anyone foresee a blocker?
Marco Rivera: None from PR side. I'll queue the outreach and stand by for the live link.
Priya Patel: Legal on standby for quick edits.
commops-bot: Reminder scheduled: 14:50 PT — send reminder to #marketing.
Ava Chen: Perfect. I'll be on the launch call 14:55-15:30 PT. Please ping if anything slips. :rocket:

source.text:
Ava Chen: FYI I dropped a one-pager draft in the shared drive focused on enterprise deployment decisions (security, SLAs, and short benchmark claims). Link: https://docs.google.com/enterprise-onepager-draft

Marco Diaz: reading now. We should decide which latency metric to highlight on the one-pagerp95 or p99? Sales usually wants p95.

Ava Chen: let's use p95 in the one-pager and keep p99 in the appendix for big customers. Example copy: "Typical p95 token latency: Hosted ~230ms, Dedicated ~180ms, Private (on-prem tuned) ~210ms."

Priya Nair: Add a short security checklist at the top: encryption at rest/in transit, KMS/HSM options, SOC2 attestation, audit logs, VPC peering. Sales asks for that immediately.

Sam Patel: Also include a sentence about graceful degradation: "Automatic regional fallback and model-variant routing preserve availability under regional capacity constraints." Helps with uptime questions.

Jasmine Li: I wrote two headline variants for the landing/social: 1) "Predictable inference for production" 2) "Lower inference costs, keep quality". Which should we push for webinar landing?

Marco Diaz: Go with the reliability angle for enterprise webinar. We'll A/B test the cost angle in nurture emails. Jasmine, add both to the doc and indicate the recommended webinar headline.

Ava Chen: done. Also proposing an inbound lead routing snippet for field: 
```
if lead.industry in ["finance", "healthcare"]:
  route_to = "enterprise-sales"
elif lead.sla == "SLA-required":
  route_to = "solutions-architects"
else:
  route_to = "growth-sales"
```
Engineers will validate tag mapping, but marketing needs the initial rule set.

Priya Nair: Add fallback route to "platform-ops" for infra/security-specific requests and tracking tag campaign=secure-inference-q2.

Sam Patel: Webinar runbook suggestion: 1) Why deployment mode matters (3 slides), 2) Benchmarks & demo (5 mins), 3) Security & compliance (3 slides), 4) Q&A + field handoff. Keep live demo simple  show failover to Dedicated.

Jasmine Li: I wrote a 90-second benchmark blurb for press: "In our benchmark across realistic prompt mixes, Dedicated configs cut token unit cost ~22% at steady throughput and improved p95 latency ~18% vs baseline hosted setups  delivering predictable SLAs for production apps." Should we soften?

Ava Chen: Keep the numbers but preface with "typical" and link to the full benchmark deck. Marco, Priya  check if legal needs to review.

Marco Diaz: I'll loop in Legal if they flag the public claims. Also prepping an anonymized test pack if requested (harness, configs, dataset summary).

Priya Nair: For the sales one-pager include three decision prompts: "Need strict data isolation? -> Private. Need committed throughput & low variance? -> Dedicated. Fast time-to-value? -> Hosted." Small flowchart would be perfect.

Ava Chen: Please also add a one-line email follow-up for webinar registrants. Draft: "Thanks for joining  heres a concise brief on secure inference options (Private, Dedicated, Hosted), the benchmark deck, and next steps to trial Redwood with SLA targets: [link]"

Jasmine Li: Slight edit for SDRs: "Thanks for joining  heres a concise brief on secure inference options (Private, Dedicated, Hosted), the benchmark deck, and quick steps to start a trial with SLA targets: [link]"

Sam Patel: Also want a one-slide cheat sheet for field covering common objections + suggested responses (cost, compliance, uptime). I'll draft and drop into the folder by EOD.

Ava Chen: Great. Tagging LaunchBot to schedule dry-run.

LaunchBot: Calendar event created: "Enterprise deployment launch dry-run" on 2026-04-08 10:00 PT. Attendees: Ava Chen, Marco Diaz, Priya Nair, Sam Patel, Jasmine Li. Reminder 24h before. :calendar:

source.text:
Rae Kim: quick sync — we need a tight benchmark narrative for the launch post + one hero microchart for slides. goal: make it obvious why serving choices matter to app owners. who wants to own copy vs data vs design?
Diego Santos: happy to own the data slice. recommend highlighting: median token latency, 95th tail, and normalized $/1k tokens. keep the chart uncluttered 1: single visual that pairs 95th latency (ms) with cost per 1k on a secondary axis.
Aisha Qureshi: I'll take copy. short headline + three bullets (what we measured, why it matters, how to learn more). also want a short note about "graceful fallback" behavior for reliability-conscious buyers.
Ben Carter: design here. thinking a split hero: left=big number pair (95th: 120ms, $/1k: $0.75), right=mini combo-line chart showing hosted/dedicated/private lines. variants for social, blog, slide.
Rae Kim: let's avoid exact numbers in socials unless vetted. diego, can you run a quick microbench and post the CSV? we need numbers for the slide and a one-sentence explainer per datapoint.
Diego Santos: will run microbench today. baseline config: seq=256, batch auto, KV cache warm. sample output I'll share as CSV. quick mock now: "model,mode,median_ms,95pct_ms,cost_per_1k" then rows.
Aisha Qureshi: copy draft suggestion: headline: "Serve smarter: cut latency and token cost where it counts" bullets: "We benchmarked token-level latency and per-1k token cost across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments." "Small infra changes (batching, quant) can move the needle on both user latency and monthly token bills." "Join our webinar for methodology + live Q&A." CTA: "Register: https://redwood.example/webinar-bench"
Ben Carter: mock sizes: social card 1200x628, hero 1600x600, deck 1280x720. Will deliver mock PNGs and source fig.
Rae Kim: routing note 1 where do registrations land? want a hidden field for "infra type interest" to route inbound. and tag "bench-launch".
Aisha Qureshi: inbound plan: HubSpot form -> tag=bench-launch -> immediate confirm email -> 48hr reminder -> 1hr before reminder. if email domain matches enterprise list, add to SDR queue. also capture pain point (latency/cost/reliability/security) one-click.
Diego Santos: sample CSV I'll post in channel after run. quick synthetic example for design to mock: ```model,mode,median_ms,95pct_ms,cost_per_1k
mistral-8b,hosted,22,85,0.95
mistral-8b,dedicated,18,70,0.65
mistral-8b,private,20,80,0.75
```
Ben Carter: perfect. Rae: should we use "up to" phrasing in social? keeps us safe.
Rae Kim: yes. copy rule: use "up to" or ranges and link to methodology on landing page. landing page must include seq length, batch config, quant settings.
Aisha Qureshi: landing page microcopy: short explainer of methodology + link to repo. include a small footnote: "Results vary by model, workload, and sequence length."
webhook-bot: Webinar scheduled: "Inference Benchmarks: Latency & Cost Playbook" on 2026-04-14 11:00 PDT. Reg page: https://redwood.example/webinar-bench
Diego Santos: timeline: I'll have CSV + short blurb by EOD. Ben can start mock visuals with placeholder numbers. Aisha will finalize copy once Diego confirms numbers.
Ben Carter: will prepare three mock variants and label layers for quick swaps. will add tooltip text for each datapoint: "hosted/dedicated/private; seq=256; batching=auto"
Rae Kim: final ask: what's the single quick takeaway on the hero? I'm leaning to "95th latency vs $/1k" pair as the headline metric. thoughts?
Aisha Qureshi: Agreed. that'll speak to both engineers (latency) and finance/biz (cost).
Diego Santos: agreed. I'll make sure cost is normalized per 1k tokens and latency shown as ms.
Rae Kim: thanks everyone 1 reconvene after Diego posts results. keep copy conservative and methodology transparent. :white_check_mark:

source.messages:
Maya Patel: Quick sync — proposing a combined webinar + benchmark narrative to fuel PLG funnel experiments. Top-level goal: webinar signups -> deliver starter prompt templates + 7-day free credits -> first API call within 48h. Hypothesis: starter prompt pack in the post-webinar flow increases activation rate by ~15% compared to credits-only.

Ravi Singh: Love the framing. What baseline numbers are we comparing to?

Ava Chen: Baseline from last quarter: webinar -> signup 18%, signup -> activation (1st API call) 4%. Cost per activated user ~ $420. Need to bring activation up or CAC will spike.

Deon Brooks: Thinking of a 3-arm experiment: A) credits + email w/ prompt pack, B) credits + in-console guided tour + contextual nudge, C) credits + 30min office hours slot + prompt pack. Run equal allocation, measure 7d activation and 30d usage.

Maya Patel: Timeline: run a single webinar (benchmarks + 2 live demos) week of Apr 6. Landing page + thank-you page must capture intent signals (company, estimated monthly requests, use-case). Post-webinar seq: day0 immediate email w/ prompt pack, day1 in-app nudge, day3 tips + invite.

Ravi Singh: Routing rules for inbound leads (sketch):
- if company_size >= 100 and est_requests_per_month >= 500 -> route to sales-dedicated queue
- else if company_email present and uses promo_code -> route to growth-nurture
- else -> automated nurture funnel (email + in-app)
Will create a lightweight webhook mapping for the form.

Ava Chen: We should pass the experiment arm in the lead meta so support/growth knows the variant. Also include utm_campaign and webinar_id.

Deon Brooks: Quick pseudo-rule to attach arm:
```
if webinar_rsvp and random_number < 0.333 -> arm A
elif webinar_rsvp and random_number < 0.666 -> arm B
else -> arm C
```
Add arm tag to user metadata for analytics.

onboard-bot: Reminder: Webinar "Benchmarks that Move the Needle" scheduled Thu Apr 9 10:00am PT — calendar invites sent to presenters. :bell:

Maya Patel: Messaging thread for launch post and benchmark narrative: need a short headline (tweet-style), 1-paragraph TL;DR for landing, and 3 takeaway bullets for slides. Draft headline: "How teams cut text-gen cost 3x while improving latency — live benchmarks + playbook." Thoughts?

Ravi Singh: Snappy. Maybe soften "cut cost 3x" -> "reduce per-token cost up to 3x" to avoid overclaim. Add CTA: "Reserve your seat + get a starter prompt pack"

Ava Chen: For the email copy we'll A/B subject lines: S1 = "Starter prompts + free credits — join Apr 9 webinar" S2 = "See our live benchmarks — get prompt templates & credits". We'll track open -> click -> activation funnel.

Deon Brooks: Analytics: capture cohort by arm, webinar attendance, prompt-download, credit-redemption, first API call timestamp, and 7d token usage. We'll instrument in Growth Analytics dashboard.

Maya Patel: Owners: Landing + registration form = @Ravi (draft by Fri), Post-webinar email sequence + prompt pack = @Ava (draft by Mon), Routing/webhook + arm tagging = @Deon (integration by Tue), Slides + benchmark narrative = @Maya (draft + data by Wed).

Ravi Singh: I'll spin up the landing with hidden fields for est_requests and utm params. Sharing the form link here when done.

Ava Chen: I'll draft two email variants and the prompt pack (3 starter prompts + usage notes).

Maya Patel: Decision checklist before launch: 1) form + routing tested, 2) arm assignment logic in analytics, 3) email variants uploaded, 4) in-app nudge snippet ready. We should run a soft QA with 20 internal test signups.

onboard-bot: Added task: #marketing-webinar-17698 -> "soft QA signups" assigned to Maya, deadline Tue Apr 7.

Deon Brooks: Small ops note — make sure credit issuance is idempotent (we had a double-issue bug last time). I'll add a quick guard in the webhook: issue_credit(user_id) only if user.metadata.credit_issued != true.

Maya Patel: Final ask — who can own tracking the experiment? Need daily standup updates for first week after webinar. @Deon?

Deon Brooks: I can own tracking + dashboard. Will post daily digest in #marketing for 7 days.

Maya Patel: Perfect. If no blockers, we'll lock assets by EOD Fri and run the webinar Apr 9. :rocket:

Ravi Singh: Sounds good. I'll post the landing link once up.

Ava Chen: On it — will share draft email + prompt pack for review tomorrow. :memo:

Maya Patel: Thanks team. Quick react with :thumbsup: if you agree to deadlines.

preview:
Maya Patel: Quick sync — proposing a combined webinar + benchmark narrative to fuel PLG funnel experiments. Top-level goal: webinar signups -> deliver starter prompt templates + 7-day free credits -> first API call within 48h. Hypothesis: starter prompt pack in the post-webinar flow increases activation rate by ~15% compared to credits-only.

Ravi Singh: Love the framing. What baseline numbers are we comparing to?

Ava Chen: Baseline from last quarter: webinar -> signup 18%, signup -> activation (1st API call) 4%. Cost per activated user ~ $420. Need to bring activation up or CAC will spike.

Deon Brooks: Thinking of a 3-arm experiment: A) credits + email w/ prompt pack, B) credits + in-console guided tour + contextual nudge, C) credits + 30min office hours slot + prompt pack. Run equal allocation, measure 7d activation and 30d usage.

Maya Patel: Timeline: run a single webinar (benchmarks + 2 live demos) week of Apr 6. Landing page + thank-you page must capture intent signals (company, estimated monthly requests, use-case). Post-webinar seq: day0 immediate email w/ prompt pack, day1 in-app nudge, day3 tips + invite.

Ravi Singh: Routing rules for inbound leads (sketch):
- if company_size >= 100 and est_requests_per_month >= 500 -> route to sales-dedicated queue
- else if company_email present and uses promo_code -> route to growth-nurture
- else -> automated nurture funnel (email + in-app)
Will create a lightweight webhook mapping for the form.

Ava Chen: We should pass the experiment arm in the lead meta so support/growth knows the variant. Also include utm_campaign and webinar_id.

Deon Brooks: Quick pseudo-rule to attach arm:
```
if webinar_rsvp and random_number < 0.333 -> arm A
elif webinar_rsvp and random_number < 0.666 -> arm B
else -> arm C
```
Add arm tag to user metadata for analytics.

source.thread:
Camila Reyes: Final review thread for the launch brief + internal comms for Marketplace Listing Refresh. Goal: lock today so Partnerships can submit w/ clean attachments + we can schedule #announcements post.

Doc: Launch brief vFinal in Drive (same link as the doc in the launch folder). Please reply w/ :white_check_mark: sign-off or last edits.

Sign-off checklist (please confirm):
- PMM: positioning + SKU naming + claims OK
- Partnerships/GTM: AWS + GCP submission wording/fields OK
- Docs/DevEx: final URLs + onboarding flow steps accurate + redirects in place
- Security/Compliance: security statements match packet + no over-claims
- Design: screenshots/diagrams final + correct formats

Also: internal comms draft includes (1) #announcements post, (2) enablement note for AEs/SEs, (3) support escalation snippet.

Soojin Lee: Skimmed the brief. Overall looks good. 2 nits:
1) In the “Why now” section, can we avoid “best-in-class” / “industry-leading” wording? Partner review has been touchy on that.
2) In the SKU section, make sure we consistently use: Hosted / Dedicated / Private (and then Add-ons). No “Enterprise Hosted” anywhere.

Otherwise :thumbsup: from PMM side once those two tweaks land.

Chris Osei: +1 on removing “best-in-class” — we got that note in partner feedback round 1. Camila, once you patch I’ll update the go/no-go checklist.

Jordan Blake: Partnerships view: content is solid. A couple things to keep us out of AWS/GCP review loops:
- “HIPAA-ready” phrasing: can we change to “supports HIPAA-aligned deployments” / “eligible workloads depend on customer configuration” (or just remove entirely from the marketplace-facing copy)?
- Make sure the brief references the security packet as “available on request / via docs link” depending on what we decided is public.

Otherwise I’m good to sign off after the language tweak.

Stephanie Nguyen: +1. Also internal comms for sales: can we include 1 line on when to route to private offer vs public offer? Something like:
- Public offer for quick procurement
- Private offer for committed spend / custom terms / Dedicated/Private

Would help the field a lot.

Alex Martinez: Docs sign-off pending one thing: we need the final canonical URLs in the brief + internal comms.
Right now I see 2 variants for the onboarding guide.
Please standardize to:
- /docs/marketplace/aws (AWS onboarding)
- /docs/marketplace/gcp (GCP onboarding)
- /docs/security/marketplace (security overview landing)

We have INT-771 for redirects — just want the brief to match what we’re submitting.

Elliot Price: +1 on URLs. Also small clarity edit in the “How it works” bullets:
The flow should be:
1) Subscribe in marketplace
2) Wait for entitlement propagation (can be a few minutes)
3) Sign in / create Redwood org
4) Link subscription to org
5) Create API key
6) First request + metering starts

Right now it reads like API key comes before linking. That’s not correct w/ the new entrypoint.

Naomi Feldman: Security pass: mostly fine. Please remove/soften any absolute claims like:
- “All customer data is encrypted end-to-end” (not accurate phrasing)
Use: “Encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+) and encryption at rest (cloud-managed + optional customer-managed keys where supported).”

Also make sure retention defaults are framed as “defaults” and that customers can request adjustments on Dedicated/Private (per the retention doc). After that, :white_check_mark: from me.

Tessa Morgan: Design/UX: screenshots are ready in the asset folder. Please confirm we’re only using the 3 console shots + 1 diagram:
- Usage dashboard (token cost breakdown)
- Model routing + fallback policy screen
- Audit log view
- “Subscription → Entitlement → Org Provisioning” diagram

If marketing wants the “API key created” modal screenshot too, we can add but it’s a 5th image and some marketplaces cap image slots.

Alicia Perez: +1. Formats exported as PNG (1600x900) and a 1280x720 backup set. Logos in SVG + PNG. Just need final filenames once we lock ordering.

Camila Reyes: Great catches. I’m making updates now:
- Remove “best-in-class/industry-leading”
- Standardize SKU naming to Hosted/Dedicated/Private + Add-ons
- Replace “HIPAA-ready” mention with safer language (or remove from listing copy section)
- Add the public vs private offer guidance line in internal comms
- Standardize docs URLs to Alex’s list
- Fix onboarding flow order (link subscription before API key)
- Update encryption/retention language per Naomi

Re: screenshots, let’s keep it to 4 assets (3 console + 1 diagram) for the listing. We can put the API key modal in docs instead.

Soojin Lee: Saw the updated wording in the brief just now. PMM sign-off: :white_check_mark:
One more micro-edit: in the differentiation bullets, “lowest latency” → “low latency” (no superlatives).

Jordan Blake: Partnerships sign-off with the HIPAA phrasing removed from marketplace-facing sections. :white_check_mark:
Please ensure the internal comms includes the exact attachment list we’re submitting (security overview PDF + diagram). That saves us back-and-forth with the partner portal.

Alex Martinez: Docs sign-off: :white_check_mark: once the brief links are updated (I see the /docs/marketplace/aws + /gcp now).
Also, can you add a line in comms: “If entitlement isn’t found after 15 min, use the troubleshooting doc + open support ticket.” That’s been a repeat issue.

Elliot Price: +1. Docs sign-off from me too after that line is included. :white_check_mark:

Naomi Feldman: Security sign-off after the encryption language change landed (looks good now). :white_check_mark:

Tessa Morgan: Design sign-off. :white_check_mark: I’ll keep the 4 images as final set.

Camila Reyes: Updates are in. Added the entitlement troubleshooting line + attachment list. Thanks all.

Final sign-off checklist status:
- PMM (Soojin): :white_check_mark:
- Partnerships (Jordan/Stephanie): :white_check_mark:
- Docs/DevEx (Alex/Elliot): :white_check_mark:
- Security (Naomi): :white_check_mark:
- Design (Tessa/Alicia): :white_check_mark:

Next steps: I’ll schedule the #announcements post for tomorrow morning + send enablement note to Sales/CS today EOD. Chris, I’ll DM you the final launch brief PDF for the tracker.

source.content:
Camila Reyes: Need to lock screenshot plan for AWS/GCP listings today so Alicia can start comps. What are the must-have console shots? Also: who’s capturing vs approving?

Soojin Lee: +1. Marketplace pages usually allow ~5–8 screenshots but we should aim for 6 max. Want them to map to our 3 modes (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) + “ops” story.

Ben Carter: From funnel data, dropoff is around entitlement/org provisioning + first key. A screenshot that shows “Create API key” and “First request” guidance could help reduce confusion.

Tessa Morgan: UX-wise we should show:
1) Marketplace entrypoint / “Link subscription” screen (w/ success state)
2) Create org / org selection (if existing org)
3) API key creation modal
4) Usage / cost dashboard
5) Model catalog / model details (pin version)
6) Rollouts / fallback policy (if we can fit)

Alicia Perez: Works for me. A few constraints:
- Need consistent aspect ratio across all shots (prefer 16:9)
- No PII, no real customer names, no internal IDs
- Consistent theme + clean sidebar (collapse if needed)
If we can consolidate #1/#2 into one shot, that helps.

Chloe Martin: I can capture from staging w/ demo org. We already have the marketplace onboarding entrypoint behind a flag; I can get:
- “Validate entitlement” screen (AWS + GCP variants)
- Success state (entitlement confirmed)
- Common error state + retry copy (if you want)

Alex Martinez: Re: docs alignment — would love at least one screenshot that matches the onboarding guide steps exactly (subscription → link → API key). If the listing screenshot differs from docs UI, support tickets spike.

Elliot Price: +1. Also please avoid screenshots with “beta/experimental” labels. Marketplace reviewers get twitchy.

Camila Reyes: Ok, let’s decide final set. Proposed 6:
A) Console home / Overview (latency + token cost)
B) Model Catalog (with verified profiles + “pin version”)
C) Marketplace onboarding: link subscription / entitlement verified
D) Create API key (and “copy key”)
E) Usage dashboard (metering, spend, quotas)
F) Rollouts (canary + fallback) OR Audit logs (enterprise)
Thoughts on F?

Soojin Lee: For marketplace buyers, “Audit logs” is a stronger enterprise proof point than rollouts. Rollouts are nice but less procurement-driven. I’d pick Audit Logs if we only get one.

Ben Carter: Agree. Rollouts is more for product folks. Audit logs + export to SIEM is what I keep hearing in onboarding calls.

Tessa Morgan: If we do Audit Logs, make sure it’s readable (events list + filters) and doesn’t look like a raw admin page. We can frame it as “Governance & auditability.”

Alicia Perez: Audit Logs screenshot is also visually distinct (table view). Good for variety.

Chloe Martin: I can capture Audit Logs page in demo org. Need a clean event set (login, key created, model deploy, etc.) — I can generate a few events in staging.

Camila Reyes: Great. Ownership:
- Capture: Chloe (console), Ben to sanity-check “funnel step” accuracy
- Visual polish / callouts: Alicia
- UX review: Tessa
- Copy/caption approval: Soojin
- Docs consistency check: Alex + Elliot

Soojin Lee: Captions: keep them benefit-forward, not UI-forward. Example: “Link your marketplace subscription in minutes” vs “Entitlement screen.” I’ll draft 1-liners per screenshot.

Alicia Perez: Please send me raw PNGs + the URL/state used, so I can recreate if we need updates. Also specify if AWS/GCP requires different variants.

Chloe Martin: Question: do we need separate AWS vs GCP entitlement screenshot? UI is basically the same except provider label.

Camila Reyes: Let’s do one generic “Marketplace subscription linked” that doesn’t show provider-specific logo (if possible). If not possible, we’ll pick AWS for AWS listing and GCP for GCP listing.

Elliot Price: If we do provider-specific, double-check the provider naming matches partner portal language. (AWS is picky about “AWS Marketplace” vs “AWS marketplace”.)

Ben Carter: Also: blur any org IDs / subscription IDs. Those show up in the entitlement payload in some states.

Chloe Martin: Yep, I’ll capture in a state that doesn’t display IDs. If unavoidable, I’ll blur in post or use devtools to hide before capture.

Tessa Morgan: For consistency: same browser zoom (100%), same window width. Don’t crop too tight; leave room for Alicia’s callouts.

Alicia Perez: Specs reminder:
- 1920x1080 PNG preferred
- Dark mode OFF (use light theme)
- No red error banners unless it’s the intentional “retry guidance” shot (but we’re not using that for listing)

Camila Reyes: Timeline: need first pass by EOD tomorrow for PMM review + partner submission packet.

Chloe Martin: I can deliver raw set tomorrow 2pm PT.

Soojin Lee: Works. I’ll review captions same day.

Alex Martinez: Once we have finals, I’ll reference the same shots in the onboarding docs (or at least ensure they match). Please don’t change UI labels last minute :)

Alicia Perez: I’ll create a Figma frame for “Marketplace listing screenshots” and drop comps there. Share link when ready.

Camila Reyes: Perfect. :thumbsup: Dropping this in the asset checklist doc after this thread.

Ben Carter: :thumbsup:

Elliot Price: :thumbsup: (and ty for keeping docs in the loop)

Alicia Perez: Figma link incoming tomorrow AM.

Chloe Martin: Cool, I’ll start capturing right after standup.

Camila Reyes: Dropping the AWS Marketplace listing copy updates here for review. Goal is to align to new positioning + avoid any spicy competitive claims that get flagged in partner review. Need PMM + Partnerships sign-off today/tomorrow.

Camila Reyes: Proposed headline options (pick 1):
1) Redwood Inference — Production-grade LLM inference (Hosted, Dedicated, Private)
2) Run and optimize LLM inference across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments
3) High-performance LLM inference with cost + latency controls

Camila Reyes: Above-the-fold blurb draft:
“Redwood Inference is a developer-first platform for running and optimizing large language model inference across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments. Ship LLM features with predictable latency, throughput, and reliability—with built-in observability, rollouts, and cost controls.”

Camila Reyes: Feature bullets draft:
• Hosted API: streaming chat/text, embeddings, and reranking with simple REST/SDK
• Dedicated: reserved GPU capacity for predictable throughput and data isolation
• Private: VPC / on‑prem deployment options for regulated workloads
• Optimize: batching, caching, and quantization recommendations to reduce unit cost
• Console: token-level cost + latency breakdowns, tracing hooks, and error analysis

Camila Reyes: FAQ snippet (procurement):
“Can I purchase via private offer?” → Yes, AWS Private Offers available for enterprise terms/discounts.

Camila Reyes: Two lines I’m unsure about (might be too absolute):
A) “Best-in-class latency”
B) “Up to 50% lower cost than other providers”

Ben Carter: +1 on removing A/B. AWS reviewers are super picky on unverifiable superlatives. Suggest we swap to: “Low-latency inference with workload-aware batching and caching.”

Ben Carter: Also for bullets: can we add something more concrete on reliability? Maybe: “Graceful fallback policies to compatible model variants/regions when capacity is constrained.” (assuming we’re comfortable).

Soojin Lee: Agree w/ Ben — please no “best-in-class” or comparative % unless we cite a public benchmark inside the listing (we’re not doing that in marketplace).

Soojin Lee: Edits suggested:
- Headline: Option 1 is safest/clearest for procurement (“Production-grade” + the 3 modes)
- Blurb: Replace “predictable latency” with “configurable latency/cost controls” (predictable can read like a guarantee)

Soojin Lee: Reword blurb to:
“Redwood Inference is a developer-first platform for running and optimizing LLM inference across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments. Ship to production with configurable latency/cost controls, model rollouts, and observability built for serving.”

Stephanie Nguyen: Partnerships POV: keep “AWS Marketplace” mention in the first 2 lines somewhere (procurement folks skim). Something like:
“Available in AWS Marketplace for streamlined procurement and consolidated billing.”

Stephanie Nguyen: Also careful on “on‑prem” in the AWS listing — it can trigger additional questions. We can say “Private deployment options (VPC; on‑prem available on request)” or keep it as “VPC/private networking options” depending on what Legal wants.

Jordan Blake: +1 on procurement language early. The main win here is “buy via marketplace, start fast.”

Jordan Blake: For the FAQ, add one about “How does billing work?” with a simple line: “Usage is metered and billed through AWS Marketplace (dimensions depend on the SKU).” No need to go deep.

Chris Osei: For consistency with the PRD + SKU work: can we make sure we say “Hosted / Dedicated / Private” (capitalized) everywhere? Also avoid mixing “Dedicated capacity” vs “reserved GPU pools” in the same section.

Camila Reyes: Got it. Proposed revised top section (incorporating feedback):
Headline: “Redwood Inference — Production-grade LLM inference (Hosted, Dedicated, Private)”
Subhead: “Available in AWS Marketplace for streamlined procurement and consolidated billing.”
Blurb:
“Redwood Inference is a developer-first platform for running and optimizing LLM inference across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments. Ship to production with configurable latency/cost controls, model rollouts, and observability built for serving.”

Camila Reyes: Revised bullets (v2):
• Hosted: streaming chat/text, embeddings, and reranking via simple REST/SDK
• Dedicated: reserved GPU capacity for predictable throughput and data isolation
• Private: deployment options for regulated workloads (VPC; on‑prem available on request)
• Optimize: recommendations for batching, caching, and quantization to reduce unit cost
• Console: token-level cost + latency breakdowns, dashboards, and tracing hooks
• Reliability: routing and fallback policies to maintain availability during capacity constraints

Ben Carter: Looks good. On Dedicated bullet: “predictable throughput” is fine IMO (not a hard SLA claim) but “predictable latency” would be riskier.

Soojin Lee: Minor copy nit: change “predictable throughput” → “consistent throughput” (less promise-y). Otherwise PMM approved.

Stephanie Nguyen: Approved from Partnerships with that tweak. Also pls remove any mention of competitor names anywhere (even in FAQ). AWS sometimes flags it.

Camila Reyes: Done — swapped to “consistent throughput.” Will push v4 into the AWS listing doc + tag for final check in the partner tracker.

Jordan Blake: :thumbsup: ship it. If AWS asks for proof on the reliability/fallback line we can point to “automatic fallback policies” in the docs, but wording here seems safe.

Chris Osei: Logging approvals: PMM (Soojin) + Partnerships (Stephanie) approved copy v4. Next step: update the draft listing doc + attach to submission checklist.

Camila Reyes: Updated in the draft. Linking here when ready: (internal) aws-listing-copy-draft-v3 → v4 section on top. Thanks all.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — GA last-click and Marketo MQL counts are diverging this week. GA shows 1,200 leads, Marketo shows 1,050. Anyone digging?
Miguel Torres: Saw that this morning. Biggest gap looks like the webinar source. GA has 420 webinar regs, Marketo has 360. Suspect UTM mapping.
Priya Patel: I pulled the dashboard and the campaign param changed after the launch post (utm_campaign=rw_launch_v2 vs rw_launch). Forms prefill might be dropping the UTM.
Samir Gupta: @Priya can you paste the query output so I can check submit vs pageview?
Priya Patel: Query snippet: ```SELECT source, count(*) as regs FROM event_signups WHERE date >= '2026-03-14' GROUP BY source;``` results: organic 300, paid_social 200, webinar 420, direct 280. Marketo import shows webinar 360.
AnalyticsBot: New dashboard snapshot saved: https://analytics.redwood.ai/dashboards/weekly-growth (saved by Priya) :white_check_mark:
Priya Patel: Marketo is stripping utm_campaign when the form prefill runs. Will add a hidden field mapping to preserve utm_campaign on submit.
Lars Berg: Also the Looker explore join uses ga.client_id vs marketo.lead_id. We should fallback to email join for these cross-platform counts until we normalize ids.
Ava Chen: Good call. For the weekly KPI slide we should show both GA and Marketo counts side-by-side and a short note on attribution method. Miguel, can you update the dashboard before EOD?
Miguel Torres: On it. I'll add a comparison table and a small legend explaining "last_click (GA)" vs "lead_submit (Marketo)".
Samir Gupta: Also add a cohort card: webinar registrants -> attendees -> MQL. Early numbers show 28% attended, 9% -> MQL. That will help budget asks.
Priya Patel: If conversion to MQL stays ~9%, we can justify shifting +15% of sponsored budget to webinar promotion.
Ava Chen: Action list: Miguel update dashboard; Priya patch Marketo form mapping; Samir add cohort card; Lars to document id-join decision. Check-in tomorrow 10:00am in #marketing. :rocket:
AnalyticsBot: Reminder — weekly-metrics review tomorrow 10:00 in #marketing. Agenda uploaded.

title: #marketing: 1769001234
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — GA last-click and Marketo MQL counts are diverging this week. GA shows 1,200 leads, Marketo shows 1,050. Anyone digging?
Miguel Torres: Saw that this morning. Biggest gap looks like the webinar source. GA has 420 webinar regs, Marketo has 360. Suspect UTM mapping.
Priya Patel: I pulled the dashboard and the campaign param changed after the launch post (utm_campaign=rw_launch_v2 vs rw_launch). Forms prefill might be dropping the UTM.
Samir Gupta: @Priya can you paste the query output so I can check submit vs pageview?
Priya Patel: Query snippet: ```SELECT source, count(*) as regs FROM event_signups WHERE date >= '2026-03-14' GROUP BY source;``` results: organic 300, paid_social 200, webinar 420, direct 280. Marketo import shows webinar 360.
AnalyticsBot: New dashboard snapshot saved: https://analytics.redwood.ai/dashboards/weekly-growth (saved by Priya) :white_check_mark:
Priya Patel: Marketo is stripping utm_campaign when the form prefill runs. Will add a hidden field mapping to preserve utm_campaign on submit.

source.body:
Ben Carter: putting together the FINAL sprint readout (Week 4) today. need inputs by 3pm PT so Camila can review before we share tomorrow.

Ben Carter: doc will follow the standard template + 1-slide exec summary. pls drop:
1) what shipped (links)
2) results (best estimate + caveats)
3) decision (keep/iterate/kill)
4) follow-ons

Ben Carter: draft readout doc (WIP): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lifecycle-growth-final-readout-wip
Ben Carter: funnel dashboard: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/activation-funnel-q1

Tyler Benson: for paid/attribution section, here’s the Week 3–4 rollup (post-UTM cleanup):
- Spend cap held: ~$18.5k total (under $20k)
- Best audience: signup-no-activation (1–7d lookback)
- CPA to “first successful request”: ~12–15% lower vs broad site visitors (varies by platform)
- Creative: “Ship your first request in 5 minutes” headline consistently beat the “latency/cost” one on CTR

Tyler Benson: caveat: attribution is directional; we’re relying on UTMs + last-touch in HubSpot. will add in readout as such.

Sergio Costa: dashboard note: numbers look stable after the dedupe fix (PR-28521). I pulled a snapshot for the readout timeframe (Jan 14 → Feb 7) and saved panels here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1activation-dashboard-screenshots

Sergio Costa: biggest shifts:
- signup → api_key_created improved slightly after console nudge rollout
- api_key_created → first_success improved most from email v2 cohort

Paula Mendes: +1, but please include the footnote: pre-fix (before Jan 24) signup and first_success were inflated in one region due to duplicate event emission. after fix we re-baselined. if anyone asks “why did activation drop overnight”, that’s why.

Paula Mendes: proposed metric phrasing for readout:
- Primary: % of new signups reaching first_success within 7d
- Secondary: % reaching 100k_tokens within 7d, TTFT (p50), D7 active usage

Paula Mendes: also: we should call out that D30 is not fully mature for later cohorts yet, so it’s trend-only.

Marissa Cole: console nudges section draft:
Shipped:
- new nudge component + targeting/holdout (behind `lifecycle_growth_nudges_v1`)
- 4 nudge variants tested: create API key, run first request, enable streaming, view usage dashboard
Results:
- CTR strongest: API key + First request (streaming was meh; usage dashboard got clicks but low downstream)
Decision:
- propose graduating API key + First request nudges to always-on onboarding for self-serve Hosted API
- keep streaming + usage dashboard behind flag for iteration (copy + placement)

Marissa Cole: link to spec: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Console+Onboarding+Nudges+Framework

Dylan Brooks: eng addendum: dismissal persistence bug is fixed (the “appears on wrong page” edge case). flag targeting is now by cohort assignment + region guard.

Dylan Brooks: for readout, suggest we explicitly state holdout: 10% holdout for nudges, 5% holdout for email (so we can keep learning without stopping everything).

Soojin Lee: messaging notes for final narrative:
- lead with “get to first working request fast” (time-to-value)
- then “predictable latency + cost” as the reasons to stick around
- avoid over-claiming “fastest” or hard promises in lifecycle touches; use “optimized for production” / “Redwood-verified profiles”

Soojin Lee: also recommend standardizing “first successful request” wording across email + console + landing pages (some variants said “first API call”). small thing but it shows up in support questions.

Tessa Morgan: creative / assets status:
- email template refresh shipped (mobile spacing + CTA button styles). DES-311 closed.
- retargeting kit shipped (smaller “developer-first” variant set requested in thread 1768680044). exports are in the same Drive folder.

Tessa Morgan: if we want a follow-on experiment: add a single graphic/diagram in Email 2 (API key → hello world → usage dashboard) — could reduce confusion for folks who haven’t done this before.

Jordan Blake: reminder for readout: call out the retargeting guardrails + what we DIDN’T do.
- excluded active opps + enterprise domains (weekly refresh)
- excluded regulated segments where tracking consent is unclear
- no targeting of known POCs

Jordan Blake: sales feedback: fewer “why am I seeing ads while in procurement” pings than last quarter. please keep that sentence in there so we don’t regress.

Camila Reyes: for the exec summary, I want 3 bullets:
1) what moved the primary KPI (and by how much, even if it’s a range)
2) what we’re making always-on vs stopping
3) the top 3 follow-ons + resourcing ask (if any)

Camila Reyes: and make the caveats explicit (event fix, D30 maturity). we should be proud but precise.

Ben Carter: super helpful, thx all.

Ben Carter: here’s the structure I’m putting in the readout (so you know what I’ll quote):
- What shipped (email v2 + console nudges + retargeting + instrumentation/dashboard)
- Results summary (activation + TTFT + early retention signals)
- Guardrails status (deliverability / opt-out / audience exclusions)
- Decisions
- Follow-ons

Ben Carter: preliminary “decision” draft (pls react if you disagree):
KEEP:
- Email v2 winner variant for new signups (and expand to stalled-users segment next)
- Always-on console nudges: API key + First request
ITERATE:
- Streaming nudge (placement + better proof/benefit)
- Retargeting: focus budget on signup-no-activation + tighten creative to “first request”
KILL/PAUSE:
- broad site visitor retargeting (too noisy + worse CPA)

Ben Carter: follow-ons I’m listing (top candidates):
1) stalled-user rescue path (no api_key in 24h / no first_request in 72h) — email + console
2) onboarding checklist v1 (2–3 steps max) in console, tied to same events
3) landing page/quickstart alignment (make the “hello world” snippet the canonical CTA everywhere)

Ben Carter: if anyone has strong opinions on order/owners, reply here. otherwise I’ll finalize and send to Camila for review at ~3:30pm PT.

Tyler Benson: +1 on pausing broad visitor retargeting. I’ll add a short note on why (attribution noise + higher support risk).

Paula Mendes: decisions look right. I’ll drop the exact metric definitions + the “post-dedupe” rebaseline note into the doc.

Marissa Cole: ordering works. I can own the checklist follow-on (product) if we keep it lightweight + behind a flag.

Camila Reyes: approved direction. just make sure the exec slide has numbers in ranges and the caveat line. :thumbsup:

Ben Carter: awesome. locking by EOD. thanks everyone.
